EDCS-71000788.translation: Prostatai of Demeter: Gaius Orkeos son of Gaius, Gaius Solpicius son of Lucius, Lucius Caullius son of Lucius, Chairetios son of Chairetios, Lykon son of Nympon.
EDCS-71000788.summary: This inscription lists the names of several individuals who served as 'prostatai' (leaders or patrons) of the cult of Demeter, likely in a Greek-speaking community under Roman rule.

EDCS-71000789.translation: Here lay Montanus, a physician, and a farmer, who died aged twenty-eight, recently leaving behind his wedded wife and infant children, and his parents mourning and tearing their grey hair, because they buried him, still in the bloom of youth, his spirit departing; his father erected this out of piety.
EDCS-71000789.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Montanus, a physician and farmer who died at the age of 28, emphasizing his youth, his family's grief, and his father's piety in erecting the monument.

EDCS-73200005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Marcus Canolius Eros, likely a freedman given the name Eros, possibly from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-73400293.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Decia Daphne made this monument with her children for Claudius Parthenius, her sweetest and incomparable husband.
EDCS-73400293.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Decia Daphne and her children to her husband, Claudius Parthenius, and is likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73400294.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Asia, his sister, made this in memory for Aurelius Diodorus, her sweetest brother, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-73400294.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Asia in memory of her brother, Aurelius Diodorus, who lived for 30 years, and is likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300054.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, possibly identifying the deceased as Anneus Leptiminus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300055.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Leptiminus Domesticus made the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Fortunatus Leptiminus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300058.translation: Of Marcus Octavius Papilio, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-75300058.summary: This inscription names Marcus Octavius Papilio, a freedman of Marcus, possibly identifying him as the owner of the property or the deceased, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76300111.translation: Hail, Publius Clodius Cornelianus, dutiful one.
EDCS-76300111.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or dedication addressed to Publius Clodius Cornelianus, hailing him as dutiful, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76300112.translation: Marcus Cocceius Nicephorus, good and blameless, hail. He lived for 3 years.
EDCS-76300112.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Marcus Cocceius Nicephorus, who lived for 3 years, and hails him as good and blameless, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76300127.translation: Licinius Isaurus, while alive, made (this) for himself and his own.
EDCS-76300127.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Licinius Isaurus commissioned his own tomb while still alive, for himself and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76300128.translation: Roufeina Marcia, who lived 35 years. Sossis, her husband, made this monument for his most pious wife.
EDCS-76300128.summary: A funerary monument erected by a husband for his wife, dating to the Roman period based on the names.

EDCS-76300130.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Daphne Lykeia lived for 33 years.
EDCS-76300130.summary: An epitaph dedicated to the Chthonic Gods, recording the lifespan of Daphne Lykeia, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-76300162.translation: Septikis Horais, good and blameless, lived for 55 years, 3 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-76300162.summary: A funerary epitaph for Septikis Horais, recording her virtuous life and precise age, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-76300165.translation: [Name] made for himself and for Rowskia Antonia, his wife.
EDCS-76300165.summary: An inscription recording the commissioning of a monument by a man for himself and his wife, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-76400011.translation: Autekheion, good and blameless, lived for forty years and five months.
EDCS-76400011.summary: A funerary epitaph for Autekheion, recording his lifespan, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-76400012.translation: Kardaman, good and blameless, lived for eighty years and nine months.
EDCS-76400012.summary: A funerary epitaph for Kardaman, recording his lifespan, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-76400191.translation: Under the consulship of Gaius Cestius Macer, and of the attendant Marcus Servilius Nonianus, and of the attendant in Syracuse of Aulus Valerius Arabicus, and of the Paides of Lucius Bebius Cladus, and of the priestess of the Paides Clodia Polla, in the month of Panamos, the 26th. They came to the Paides with Aulia, daughter of Titus, Fabia, daughter of Sfongos, and mother Fabilla, and Apia, on the 13th of the month, rejoicing and giving thanks to Apollo and the Paides.
EDCS-76400191.summary: A complex inscription recording a religious festival in Syracuse involving various officials and family members, possibly dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE based on the names and consuls.

EDCS-76400194.translation: Lysimachus, son of Melissas, with the Paides and also with Anna the priestess, was born together with Eutropos and his companions, and with Eros the nablas.
EDCS-76400194.summary: An inscription mentioning Lysimachus, his family, religious officials (priestess Anna), and other individuals, likely related to a cultic community in Syracuse during the Roman period.

EDCS-76400197.translation: Thanksgivings to the Paides and to Anna and to Apollo, under the attendant in Syracuse of Aulus Matius Sestus, and of the priestess of the Paides, Caecilia. Those who came to the Nymphs and gave thanks, in the month of Apellaios, the third day. Sextus Aulus Markis Longus, Quintus Oppius Canacis, Quintus Ampius Crescens, [Name] Ailis Sella [Name], Quintus [Name]tianus, Gaius [Name]phocrates, Gaius [Name]metos.
EDCS-76400197.summary: A votive inscription recording thanksgivings to deities and religious figures in Syracuse, listing several individuals who participated in a ritual, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-78300644.translation: During the tenure of Amphipolos in Syracuse, Aprus Sextius Augureinus, and as priestess by the Children and Anna, Maioros Cecilia Artemitiou, the 15th year, Lucius Cornelius Aquila dedicated as a votive offering for himself and Poilla Cornelia his mother and for Mustia Volumnilla his wife, to Apollo and the Children and Anna.
EDCS-78300644.summary: This is a votive dedication to Apollo and other deities by Lucius Cornelius Aquila for himself and his family.

EDCS-80700020.translation: Let no one open this during the period of mourning; here lie Nophios and Nymphe, a blessing to the saints here.
EDCS-80700020.summary: An epitaph for Nophios and Nymphe, prohibiting disturbance during a mourning period.

EDCS-80700021.translation: Ithallas the shipowner, a Leptis Magnitanian, lies here, aged 35.
EDCS-80700021.summary: An epitaph for Ithallas, a shipowner from Leptis Magna, who died at age 35.

EDCS-80700029.translation: Publius Clodius Cornelianus, be pious, farewell.
EDCS-80700029.summary: A short epitaph wishing piety and farewell to Publius Clodius Cornelianus.

EDCS-80700030.translation: Marcus Cocceius Nicephorus, worthy and blameless, farewell, he lived for 3 years.
EDCS-80700030.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Cocceius Nicephorus, recording his short lifespan of three years.

EDCS-80700035.translation: Septicius Hora, worthy and blameless, lived 55 years, 3 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-80700035.summary: An epitaph for Septicius Hora, detailing his lifespan of 55 years, 3 months, and 10 days.

EDCS-80700038.translation: Marcus Valerius Marcarion lived 50 years, 8 months, [illegible].
EDCS-80700038.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Valerius Marcarion, recording his lifespan of 50 years and 8 months, with an illegible remainder.

EDCS-80700039.translation: Stranger, know that this is the tomb of Vettius Boos; he wasted away from a harsh illness and was buried by his mother; Tyche seems to be the judge; this stone is a witness, under which the earth is covered.
EDCS-80700039.summary: A tombstone for Vettius Boos, detailing his death from illness and mentioning his mother and the concept of Tyche (Fortune) as the overseer.

EDCS-00600739.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that may record a name or names, possibly funerary.

EDCS-09401431.translation: To IDI, the most illustrious proconsul, a most excellent duovir.
EDCS-09401431.summary: This is a dedication to a proconsul named IDI, honoring him for his service, possibly also in relation to his role as a duovir.

EDCS-76300129.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. [Name] lived for [number] years.
EDCS-76300129.summary: An epitaph dedicated to the Chthonic Gods, recording the lifespan of an individual, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-03000419.translation: In memory of Zodoris the fuller, who lived for 10 years.
EDCS-03000419.summary: This is a tombstone or memorial inscription for Zodoris the fuller, indicating his lifespan, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03000420.translation: Crispia Salvia, who lived about 45 years, to my sweetest wife. Julius Demetrius, husband, who lived with his wife for 15 years, with a willing heart.
EDCS-03000420.summary: This is a tombstone inscription from a husband, Julius Demetrius, to his wife Crispia Salvia, detailing their ages and years together, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03000421.summary: This inscription possibly refers to a staff belonging to Massa Picianensis, possibly owned by Petrus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03600008.translation: I bind Zopyrion of Mumbur, by Persephone and by the chthonic Titans and by the souls of the dead who pray to the uninitiated and by the priestesses of Demeter who pray. I bind him in his grave and his soul, so that he cannot speak. I bind him in his grave, along with his mind and soul. I bind him so that they cannot speak against him or act. Iunius Septimius Caius Acinius Marcus Annius Lucius Umbonius Marcus Nautius Marcus Rustius Lucius Nautius Umbonia.
EDCS-03600008.summary: This is a magical binding or curse text, written in Greek and Latin, invoking various deities and souls to restrain Zopyrion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03600027.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-03700327.translation: Quintus Herrius Domesticus lived 30 years, is buried here. Made by his freedman. Philetius to Rhosinia Grata, his slave, lived 13 years, is buried here. [Made] by Primitiva for her well-deserving husband and brother.
EDCS-03700327.summary: A funerary inscription for two individuals, one of whom was a slave, and possibly a third named Primitiva, with dates of life and burial noted.

EDCS-03700328.translation: Sosius Priscus for his grandmother Clodia Falconilla, daughter of Publius, wife of Quintus Pompeius Falconis.
EDCS-03700328.summary: A funerary dedication by Sosius Priscus to his grandmother, Clodia Falconilla, specifying her paternal and marital lineage.

EDCS-03700329.translation: Sosius Priscus for his father Quintus Pompeius Priscus, son of Sextus, of the tribe Quirina.
EDCS-03700329.summary: A funerary dedication by Sosius Priscus to his father, Quintus Pompeius Priscus, noting his filiation and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-03700332.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Xenodora made [this] for her most devoted husband, Marcus Luccius Onesimus.
EDCS-03700332.summary: A funerary dedication made by a wife, Iulia Xenodora, to her husband, Marcus Luccius Onesimus.

EDCS-05000364.translation: Pontius lived in peace more or less 30 years. He departed on the 16th day before the Kalends of June, in the consulship of the very distinguished Opilio, in the second indiction.
EDCS-05000364.summary: A funerary inscription providing the lifespan, death date, and the consular year (indicating a date in the 3rd or 4th century CE).

EDCS-06100247.translation: Cottia Euphrosyne set up [this monument] at her own expense for her most devoted daughter, Cerrinia Cottia, daughter of Lucius. The site was granted by decree of the decurions, and for their dedication she distributed denarii.
EDCS-06100247.summary: A funerary monument set up by a mother for her daughter, detailing the granting of the burial plot and a distribution of money, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-06100260.summary: A short funerary inscription stating the name and lifespan of the deceased.

EDCS-06100261.summary: A short funerary inscription stating the name and lifespan of the deceased.

EDCS-06100262.translation: To the departed spirits. Asinia Charmosyne lived 65 years and 7 months. Mallianus, her son, made [this].
EDCS-06100262.summary: A funerary inscription detailing the lifespan of Asinia Charmosyne and stating it was made by her son, Mallianus.

EDCS-06100265.translation: To Rustia Festa, pious, aged 13 years. Caius Rustius Heortus, father. Alfia Anthe, mother. Publicia Eucarpia, grandmother.
EDCS-06100265.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Rustia Festa, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-06100276.translation: To Publius Iunius Serverus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, his most dutiful wife dedicated this to him and their descendants.
EDCS-06100276.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, Publius Iunius Severus, and their descendants.

EDCS-06100283.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aulus Tedius, son of Aulus. Prefect. Tiberius Qu...
EDCS-06100283.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Aulus Tedius, who held the rank of prefect, with the dedicant being Tiberius Qu.

EDCS-06100284.translation: Betilienus Locus, of the Roman equestrian order. Locus Roscianus Antiochus, an excellent man. Locus Bassus, an excellent man. Locus Dexippianus Alfianus, an excellent man. Locus Aurelius Fronto, quaestor of the grain supply. Locus Alfianus and Pilatus. Locus Cestianus Faretrus. Locus, the procurator's place.
EDCS-06100284.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, primarily distinguished men and officials, with multiple instances of the name 'Locus', possibly indicating property boundaries or family associations, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-06100287.translation: Lucius Clodius Antiochus Eutuchus, freedman, made this.
EDCS-06100287.summary: This is a simple dedication made by a freedman named Lucius Clodius Antiochus Eutuchus.

EDCS-06100288.translation: To Antiochus, most dutiful, well-deserving. His wife made this. He lived 38 years, 7 months, 11 days.
EDCS-06100288.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a wife to her husband Antiochus, providing details of his lifespan.

EDCS-06100290.translation: To Alb Pompeius Marcellus, his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-06100290.summary: This is an inscription honoring Alb Pompeius Marcellus as a well-deserving patron.

EDCS-06100295.translation: To Aulus Mevius Zethus, patron, by his will.
EDCS-06100295.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Aulus Mevius Zethus, a patron, made according to his will.

EDCS-08300354.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Abdalas, chief of the ovium, who lived well in office for 80 years.
EDCS-08300354.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Abdalas, who served as a 'magister magnus ovium' (chief shepherd or similar role) for 80 years.

EDCS-08400264.translation: Peace be upon Israel. I, Aurelius Samohil, purchased this memorial for myself and my wife Lasia Erina, who completed her fate on the 12th day before the Kalends of November, on a Friday, the eighth day of the moon, Merobaudes and Saturninus being consuls. She lived 23 years. With peace, I adjure you by the victories that rule, and I adjure you by the honors of the patriarchs, and I adjure you by the law which the Lord gave to the Jews: let no one open this memorial and place another body upon our bones. If anyone does, may he be subject to a fine of ten pounds of silver to the treasury.
EDCS-08400264.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument with a strong religious and legal warning against disturbing the graves, written in Latin with Hebrew elements, likely from the 4th century CE given the consular dating.

EDCS-08900397.translation: By decree of the town councillors, a place for Marcus Iulius Dionysus.
EDCS-08900397.summary: This inscription records a public space granted by decree to Marcus Iulius Dionysus.

EDCS-08900398.translation: By decree of the town councillors, a place publicly given to Publius Lollius, aged 35.
EDCS-08900398.summary: This inscription records a public space granted by decree to Publius Lollius, aged 35.

EDCS-08900404.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ave Vecilia.
EDCS-08900404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vecilia, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-08900406.translation: Elpis, freedwoman of Alfena Sextus. Publius Alfenus Colax, freedman of Publius, lived for 2 years and ...
EDCS-08900406.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Elpis and Publius Alfenus Colax, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-08900407.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Ioannes.

EDCS-09300940.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Carpion, dearest son, taken from me by fate. Carpion my father. He lived, incomparable to anyone, for 20 years.
EDCS-09300940.summary: This funerary inscription, set up by a father, laments his son Carpion who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09300941.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Ostrius Vesonianus lived for 36 years, 3 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-09300941.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Ostrius Vesonianus.

EDCS-09400438.translation: Tertius made this for his father, Gnaeus Caninius Anicetus.
EDCS-09400438.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Tertius to his father, Gnaeus Caninius Anicetus.

EDCS-09400439.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Edusius Basilides lived for 45 years.
EDCS-09400439.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Publius Edusius Basilides.

EDCS-09400440.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cassia Galene lived for 40 years. Gaius Iulius Macedo made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-09400440.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Cassia Galene and was made by her husband, Gaius Iulius Macedo.

EDCS-09401425.translation: Acilius R. [dedicated this] with his own money.
EDCS-09401425.summary: This is a dedication by Acilius R. using his own funds.

EDCS-10900638.summary: This is a record of the construction of a building by Vivianus.

EDCS-10900639.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Africanus.

EDCS-10900640.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Caius Alfius Cel.

EDCS-10900642.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Antonius Iulianus.

EDCS-10900643.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aquillius Agathon lived for 50 years.
EDCS-10900643.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld for Aquillius Agathon, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-10900645.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caecinia Sozusa lived for 50 years.
EDCS-10900645.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld for Caecinia Sozusa, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-10900647.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Claud. Fortu.

EDCS-10900655.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Ignatia Athanais, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-10900656.translation: Publius made this for his freedman Lesbius and freedman Damophilus from his own property.
EDCS-10900656.summary: This is a dedication made by Publius for his freedmen, Lesbius and Damophilus.

EDCS-10900659.summary: This is a tombstone for a young girl named Publicia Euplea, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-10900662.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Trebonia Modia, daughter of Caius, lived 11 years and 3 months. Caius Trebonius Zmaragdus and Modia Hesione made this for their most devoted daughter.
EDCS-10900662.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Trebonia Modia, with her parents Caius Trebonius Zmaragdus and Modia Hesione identified as the dedidants.

EDCS-10900663.translation: Decimus Vibius Claudius Tuscus, son of Caius.
EDCS-10900663.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Decimus Vibius Claudius Tuscus.

EDCS-10900670.translation: Aponius lived 25 years. Her father made this for his daughter.
EDCS-10900670.summary: This funerary inscription states that Aponius lived 25 years and was commemorated by her father.

EDCS-10900684.summary: This inscription records the length of a section of the Aqua Cornelia, likely a public work.

EDCS-12700064.translation: Here lies, in good memory, Albina, an honorable woman who lived more or less 60 years; she rested in peace on the fourth day before the Kalends of February, and lies with her husband, of happy memory.
EDCS-12700064.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Albina, an honorable woman who lived approximately 60 years and died on January 29th, resting with her husband.

EDCS-12700066.translation: To Marina, most faithful lady, woman of wonderful wisdom, [wife] of Patricius Theodulus, in peace on the Kalends of March.
EDCS-12700066.summary: This dedicatory inscription, likely funerary, honors Marina, wife of Patricius Theodulus, and records her death on March 1st.

EDCS-12700067.summary: This inscription simply identifies Flavius Pacius as a veteran.

EDCS-12700068.translation: Lucrosa, may she be in peace.
EDCS-12700068.summary: This is a short epitaph wishing peace for Lucrosa.

EDCS-12700069.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Adeudatus.

EDCS-12700071.translation: Felix the faithful bought a place for myself from the deacon in peace.
EDCS-12700071.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Felix, identified as faithful, records the purchase of a burial plot from a deacon.

EDCS-12700073.translation: Auxentius the Spaniard, a bishop by birth, lies here, and I adjure you who read this to ask for what I have received.
EDCS-12700073.summary: This epitaph commemorates Auxentius, a bishop from Hispania, who requests readers to pray for him.

EDCS-12700074.translation: For Iulius Innocens, who lived 3 years, 9 months, 6 days, and 10 hours. Innocen made this.
EDCS-12700074.summary: This inscription records the brief life of Iulius Innocens, who died at the age of 3 years, 9 months, and 6 days.

EDCS-12700075.translation: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, this tomb for Anastasius the banker.
EDCS-12700075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anastasius, identified as a banker ('trapezita'), placed 'in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ'.

EDCS-12700077.translation: To my most holy wife Zosima the faithful, who lived 26 years and died on the 11th day before the Kalends of October, during the consulship of Amantius and Albinus.
EDCS-12700077.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to Zosima, the wife of the deceased, who died at the age of 26 during the consulship of Amantius and Albinus.

EDCS-12700078.translation: Here rests in peace of good memory Busiridis, on the 7th day before the Kalends of September, in the seventh indiction, in the consulship of Probus. Here rests in peace of good memory Serpentius, who was also laid to rest on the 10th day before the Kalends of June, in the tenth indiction, in the consulship of Agapitus.
EDCS-12700078.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Busiridis and Serpentius, providing their death dates, indictions, and consulates, indicating a late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-12800326.translation: Above the little plot where I, a virgin named Zoe, aged five, lie. January, the sixth month. Sworn by God and the infernal deities: let no one dare to open this grave during life or after death.
EDCS-12800326.summary: This epitaph is for a young virgin named Zoe, who died at the age of five, and includes a curse against anyone disturbing her grave.

EDCS-13600457.translation: Flame-fleeing brothers, the greatest gifts of piety, whom hostility took away, Merulus, a most illustrious man and the notable ex-governor of the province of Sicily, has restored.
EDCS-13600457.summary: This inscription records the restoration and dedication of a monument or offerings by Merulus, a high-ranking official, to 'flame-fleeing brothers' previously lost due to hostility.

EDCS-13800099.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely identifying a person named Boniface, possibly the fourth of that name.

EDCS-13900105.translation: For Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, a stone tomb.
EDCS-13900105.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking a stone tomb for Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius.

EDCS-13900509.translation: To our Imperial Augusti, from the workshop of Caius Attius Stocius of the Porcian family.
EDCS-13900509.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors from a workshop, likely belonging to a freedman named Caius Attius Stocius.

EDCS-13900569.translation: The memory of Martinus the carpenter, who was of the patrician class.
EDCS-13900569.summary: This inscription records the memory of Martinus, a carpenter associated with the patrician class.

EDCS-13900570.translation: The memory of Laurentius the doctor and his wife Prosdocia.
EDCS-13900570.summary: This inscription commemorates Laurentius, a doctor, and his wife Prosdocia.

EDCS-13900571.translation: The memory of Antonius the potter and Leontia.
EDCS-13900571.summary: This inscription records the memory of Antonius, a potter, and Leontia.

EDCS-13900572.translation: Burial place for the household slaves of Palumba, formerly belonging to Hirineius.
EDCS-13900572.summary: This records a burial place for slaves belonging to Palumba, who was previously associated with Hirineius.

EDCS-14800312.summary: This is a fragmented dedication to an individual named Marcus Fulvinius, possibly a prefect.

EDCS-14805792.translation: To the departed spirits. Belleia Vesonia lived 34 years. Lucius Ostrius Crescens [erected this] to his most rare wife.
EDCS-14805792.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Belleia Vesonia, set up by her husband Lucius Ostrius Crescens.

EDCS-14805793.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Ostrius Crescens lived 76 years.
EDCS-14805793.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Lucius Ostrius Crescens.

EDCS-14805794.summary: This funerary inscription records the short lifespan of Aurellius tor.

EDCS-14805795.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Saufeius Sabinus erected this to his most dutiful daughter Ofilia Sabina, daughter of Marcus, and to his wife Ofilia Ursa, and to himself, and to their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-14805795.summary: This is a family funerary inscription set up by Marcus Saufeius Sabinus for his daughter, wife, himself, and his freedmen and descendants.

EDCS-14805797.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius Eutyches made this for himself and his freedmen, and for his daughter, who lived 14 years, 5 months. May you prosper, posterity. The mint (?).
EDCS-14805797.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Ulpius Eutyches for himself, his freedmen, and his daughter, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14805798.translation: To Gaius Terentius Symphorus, freedman of Gaius Terentius Syntrophus, a surgeon in the IV Praetorian cohort, 28 years old. Atticus took care of him.
EDCS-14805798.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Terentius Symphorus, a surgeon in the Praetorian Guard, and notes that Atticus arranged his burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14805799.summary: This is a dedication of a monument or object by Primigenia to Demetrius, possibly a personal gift or memorial.

EDCS-14805800.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cytisus lived 10 years. Stellius Novellus Amaranthus made this for his well-deserving son.
EDCS-14805800.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cytisus, a child who died at age 10, set up by his father Stellius Novellus Amaranthus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14805801.translation: To Publius Avillius Pudentinus, son of Publius, who lived 11 years, 6 months. Publius Avillius Zosimus and Mevia Mereope made this for Publius Avillius Pudentinus.
EDCS-14805801.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy, Publius Avillius Pudentinus, set up by his parents or guardians, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14805803.summary: This inscription likely marks a tomb or property belonging to Lucius Avillius, created during his lifetime.

EDCS-14805809.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Sellius Atticus lived 25 years.
EDCS-14805809.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Sellius Atticus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14805812.translation: Tertius, a slave of Quintus Articuleius Paetus, made this.
EDCS-14805812.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a slave named Tertius for his master Quintus Articuleius Paetus, possibly a votive offering or memorial.

EDCS-14805813.summary: This is an incomplete inscription that likely belonged to Gnaeus Domitius Clemens, perhaps as a tombstone or ownership marker.

EDCS-15100066.translation: To our lord Salvius, Flavius Gelasius Busiris, most distinguished man, consular, the praetorium without anyone's help for so many days and months...
EDCS-15100066.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription honoring Salvius, mentioning the achievements of Flavius Gelasius Busiris, a high-ranking official, likely from late antiquity.

EDCS-15300620.summary: This is a dedication to Augusta, identified as a lamp-maker, likely from Lilybaeum.

EDCS-15500060.translation: To Gaius Julius Longus, son of Gaius, duovir, by the municipality, in honor.
EDCS-15500060.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Julius Longus, son of Gaius, a duovir, by the municipality.

EDCS-16200484.summary: This inscription contains fragmentary names, likely of an individual or individuals, with significant portions missing.

EDCS-16200824.translation: From the workshop of Porcianus, conductor of the Emperor Augustus's properties.
EDCS-16200824.summary: This inscription identifies Porcianus as a conductor managing properties of Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-16201654.translation: On the 17th day before the Kalends of September. Feridius, slave of the lady Ceres, made this pleasantly for himself. Three young men of theirs whose names are read are Onesimus, Lucius Valerius Cassianus, and Filumenus, with that woman. May they live many years. We have joined what is proper.
EDCS-16201654.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Feridius, a slave of Ceres, for himself and three young men and a woman, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-16800408.translation: From the estates of Marcus Aurelius Commodianus.
EDCS-16800408.summary: This inscription indicates property belonging to Marcus Aurelius Commodianus.

EDCS-17100042.translation: Marcus Julius Sedatus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Papiria, from Narbonne, a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, of the first century of Iuvenis, served for 14 years, lived for 32 years. Placed here.
EDCS-17100042.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Julius Sedatus, a soldier from Narbonne who served in Legio VII Gemina Felix.

EDCS-19700392.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or memorial to Lucius Gabinius.

EDCS-19700414.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or memorial to Trebius Loisios.

EDCS-19700415.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or memorial to Trebius Loisios.

EDCS-19700416.summary: This is a name, likely from a funerary monument or a personal inscription, but no further context is provided.

EDCS-19700418.summary: This is a name, likely from a funerary monument or a personal inscription, but no further context is provided.

EDCS-19800001.summary: This is a name, likely from a funerary monument or a personal inscription, but no further context is provided.

EDCS-21900295.translation: Lucius Baebius Iuncinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, prefect of the engineers, prefect of the IV Cohort of Raetians, military tribune of the XXII Legion Deiotariana, prefect of the ala of the Astyres, prefect of the roads, legal official of Egypt.
EDCS-21900295.summary: This inscription lists the impressive career of Lucius Baebius Iuncinus, a Roman official serving in various military and administrative posts, likely on a monument honoring him.

EDCS-21900296.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Candidianus, slave of our Caesar, who served in the office of the Asia province as treasurer for inheritances worth twenty.
EDCS-21900296.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Candidianus, a slave who served in a financial capacity in the province of Asia.

EDCS-21900297.translation: For Messia Crispina, daughter of Messius Priscus, priestess of the divine Augusta, from the will of her father Messius Modianus; Modianus, her brother.
EDCS-21900297.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Messia Crispina, a priestess, set up by her brother Modianus in accordance with their father's will.

EDCS-21900298.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Aemilius Felicianus, faithful public scribe, lived 43 years. Nonia Zosima to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-21900298.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aemilius Felicianus, a public scribe, erected by his wife Nonia Zosima.

EDCS-21900299.translation: Decimus Annius Rufus lived 40 years. Numitoria Hagne to her dearest husband.
EDCS-21900299.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decimus Annius Rufus, erected by his wife Numitoria Hagne.

EDCS-21900300.translation: Aristo made this for his well-deserving mother Claudia Aristene; she lived 54 years.
EDCS-21900300.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Claudia Aristene, made by her son Aristo.

EDCS-21900301.summary: This inscription records the name of Hygia, a freedwoman, likely from a tombstone or other dedicatory monument.

EDCS-21900302.translation: To the divine spirits: Annius Chrysanta to Epilantanus, his well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-21900302.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a freedman by his former master, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-21900303.translation: Hail Caius Iulius Soter! Hail Coponia Heraclia!
EDCS-21900303.summary: A brief greeting inscription possibly indicating a marriage or partnership, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900304.translation: Magullia Tyche erected this monument to Aulus Magullius Euhodus, her freedman, her patron, and her husband, who deserved exceedingly well of her.
EDCS-21900304.summary: A funerary monument erected by a freedwoman for her patron, husband, and freedman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900305.translation: Publius Nonius Primus and Pomponia Aema.
EDCS-21900305.summary: A simple naming inscription, possibly funerary or commemorative, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900308.translation: Sacred to Serapis and Isis. Gaius Ennius Secundus, a pious vow.
EDCS-21900308.summary: A votive inscription to the Egyptian deities Serapis and Isis, made by a Roman citizen, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900313.summary: An inscription recording the official career of Gaius Mevius, likely from a Roman colony or municipality during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900316.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Coelia Melpomene lived 55 years.
EDCS-21900316.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of death for Coelia Melpomene, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900317.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Nasennia Euphrosyne lived 43 years.
EDCS-21900317.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of death for Nasennia Euphrosyne, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900324.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. For Titus Aelius Pius, her son. Eutyches and Coetonis, parents. He lived 17 years, 7 months, 20 days.
EDCS-21900324.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son, set up by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900325.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Marcus Avianius Pistianus and Maria Eupraxia, living, made this for themselves and their family, and for their most dutiful son, not worthy of an earlier death, who lived 17 years, 11 months, 16 days.
EDCS-21900325.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son who died young, set up by his parents during their lifetime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21900326.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marco Mestrio Luciffrioni lived 45 years.
EDCS-21900326.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marco Mestrio Luciffrioni, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-21900327.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Onesas lived 48 years.
EDCS-21900327.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Onesas, who lived 48 years.

EDCS-21900328.translation: Quinta lived 4 years. Her mother Copia made this.
EDCS-21900328.summary: This funerary monument records the short life of Quinta and was erected by her mother Copia.

EDCS-21900329.summary: This is a simple farewell inscription for Lucius Roscius Melanio Pius.

EDCS-21900332.translation: Gaius Publicius Donatus dedicated this gift to Venus the Victorious of Hybla.
EDCS-21900332.summary: This is a dedication of a gift to the goddess Venus by Gaius Publicius Donatus.

EDCS-21900335.translation: Annia Zosima, a vow, a lamp.
EDCS-21900335.summary: This inscription records Annia Zosima's vow, possibly fulfilled with a lamp.

EDCS-21900337.translation: Quintus Lusius Laberius, proconsul, the baths.
EDCS-21900337.summary: This inscription likely records the proconsul Quintus Lusius Laberius and his connection to the baths.

EDCS-21900338.translation: To Lucius Roscius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, former prefect of the 3rd cavalry cohort, admitted by the equestrian order.
EDCS-21900338.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Roscius Rufus, detailing his military and civic career.

EDCS-21900339.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to a person named Venantius with consular rank, possibly connected to Caesar.

EDCS-21900341.summary: This inscription marks the boundary of property belonging to Vibius Severus.

EDCS-21900346.translation: Marcus Petronius Rufinus, a member of the six priests of Augustus.
EDCS-21900346.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Petronius Rufinus, who held the priesthood of the Augustales, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-21900347.translation: To Lucius Rubrius Proculus, duovir for five years, augur.
EDCS-21900347.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Rubrius Proculus, who held the office of duovir for five years and was an augur, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-21900350.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions a Claudia, who held the office of duovir, likely on a public monument.

EDCS-21900361.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Arrius Secundus lived 17 years; his fellow marble workers erected this.
EDCS-21900361.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Arrius Secundus, who died at 17, and was erected by his colleagues in the marble working trade.

EDCS-21900362.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Cornelius Campester, a merchant of cups. His wife Claudia Paezusa erected this. He lived 25 years.
EDCS-21900362.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Campester, a cup merchant, erected by his wife, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-21900363.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Gallicanus the Most Faithful, who was the steward of the Afinian estate. He lived 45 years.
EDCS-21900363.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gallicanus the Most Faithful, who served as a steward for the Afinian estate and lived 45 years.

EDCS-21900365.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Priscus lived 2 years, 2 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-21900365.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Priscus, who died at a very young age, likely indicating a child's grave.

EDCS-21900366.translation: Titus Ani Titus Aelius Caius Iulius Dio Tiberius Claudius Ce Caius Iulius Pr PAISV
EDCS-21900366.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to a collegium or group, with an incomplete or damaged final word.

EDCS-21900367.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Syriacus made this monument for himself and his family while he was alive.
EDCS-21900367.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Marcus Antonius Syriacus proactively built his own tomb for himself and his family.

EDCS-21900369.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Arrius Logismus erected this for his daughter who lived 8 months and 18 days, and also for his friend Arrius Protio, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-21900369.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Lucius Arrius Logismus for his infant daughter and his friend Arrius Protio.

EDCS-21900370.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Arrius Primillus, during his lifetime, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-21900370.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-dedicated monument by Lucius Arrius Primillus for himself and his family.

EDCS-21900371.translation: Arruntia Laudice for herself and for her husband Ariston.
EDCS-21900371.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Arruntia Laudice for herself and her husband Ariston.

EDCS-21900372.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Aulus Gami lived for 10 years.
EDCS-21900372.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Caius Aulus Gami, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-21900373.translation: Victoria lived for 4 years, 9 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-21900373.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Victoria, detailing her age in years, months, and days.

EDCS-21900374.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cabaria Euporia lived for 9 years.
EDCS-21900374.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Cabaria Euporia, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-21900375.translation: Lucius Caelius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, Macer. [---]
EDCS-21900375.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Caelius Macer.

EDCS-21900376.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Capillianus made this for Marcus Caeparius Soterichus and his daughter.
EDCS-21900376.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Capillianus for Marcus Caeparius Soterichus and his daughter.

EDCS-21900377.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Calliope lived for 8 months. Her father Lupercus made this.
EDCS-21900377.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Calliope, commemorated by her father Lupercus.

EDCS-21900381.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titus Clatius Heresistratus lived for 12 years. His father made this.
EDCS-21900381.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Titus Clatius Heresistratus, who died at the age of 12, commemorated by his father.

EDCS-21900382.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Claudia Cestia lived for 28 years. Sutoria Primilla, her most pious daughter, [made this].
EDCS-21900382.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Claudia Cestia, commemorated by her devoted daughter Sutoria Primilla.

EDCS-21900384.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Crassicius Sabinus Aptitianus lived 24 years.
EDCS-21900384.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Crassicius Sabinus Aptitianus.

EDCS-21900385.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Decimia Gamice and Decimius Agapomenus, a most devoted freedwoman.
EDCS-21900385.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a freedwoman by her patrons.

EDCS-21900386.translation: Quintus Domitius, be well; Quintus Domitius, the pious, rejoice.
EDCS-21900386.summary: A tombstone inscription with a Greek and Latin greeting to Quintus Domitius.

EDCS-21900387.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus F. Phoenix lived 30 years. May he rest in peace.
EDCS-21900387.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Titus F. Phoenix and a standard epitaph phrase.

EDCS-21900389.translation: For 76 years and 1 month, Faustina, daughter of Caius, made (this) for her pious mother.
EDCS-21900389.summary: A funerary epitaph stating the age of the deceased and identifying the dedicant as her daughter.

EDCS-21900391.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Germanus, son of Annius.
EDCS-21900391.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Germanus.

EDCS-21900392.translation: Lucius, [---], pious, [---], L. Papius Ad[---], Papia Eu[---], mother [---]
EDCS-21900392.summary: A highly fragmentary funerary inscription that likely mentions several individuals, possibly related.

EDCS-21900393.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Gaius Julius Hepmetus, Gaius Julius.
EDCS-21900393.summary: A funerary inscription mentioning Gaius Julius Hepmetus and Gaius Julius.

EDCS-21900394.translation: For 16 years and 21 days. Iulius Pancarpus and his mother Iulia Eutyxic made (this).
EDCS-21900394.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a person and identifying the dedicants as their son and mother.

EDCS-21900395.summary: A funerary inscription identifying Iulia Hesperis as the dedicator.

EDCS-21900397.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lurius Zosimus lived 7 years. Luria Melanthina made this for her son.
EDCS-21900397.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Lurius Zosimus and dedicated by his mother, Luria Melanthina.

EDCS-21900398.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Luscius Blastus made this for his mother Prima; she lived 45 years.
EDCS-21900398.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Prima, dedicated by her son Lucius Luscius Blastus.

EDCS-21900399.translation: Quinto M. Lup (dedicated this to) Quintus Man. Lup. and Domitia Pia.
EDCS-21900399.summary: A funerary epitaph listing two individuals, Quintus Man. Lup. and Domitia Pia, with a likely dedication by Quinto M. Lup.

EDCS-21900400.translation: Marcia Marulla lived 50 years. Marcia Marulla lived 50 years.
EDCS-21900400.summary: A bilingual funerary epitaph recording the life of Marcia Marulla, with text in Latin and Greek.

EDCS-21900401.translation: Neria [...] made this for herself and her family, (she lived) 9 months and [...] days.
EDCS-21900401.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely for a child, indicating the duration of life and a dedication by a parent.

EDCS-21900402.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Numonia Alexandrea lived 25 years.
EDCS-21900402.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Numonia Alexandrea.

EDCS-21900403.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nymphon and Cale, while alive, made this for themselves and their families.
EDCS-21900403.summary: A funerary epitaph recording a self-dedication by Nymphon and Cale for themselves and their families.

EDCS-21900404.summary: A short funerary epitaph or inscription with a greeting from Petilia to Eleutherio.

EDCS-21900405.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Million and Phonteia Mercatilla made this for their son Publius Phonteius, most dutiful.
EDCS-21900405.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the dedication by parents for their son, Publius Phonteius.

EDCS-21900406.translation: Of Caius Pomponius Felix and Scribonia Restituta.
EDCS-21900406.summary: A funerary epitaph listing two individuals, Caius Pomponius Felix and Scribonia Restituta.

EDCS-21900407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primae, with the name Paternus also appearing, likely the dedicant.

EDCS-21900410.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Antelus Seleucus, a devoted father, made this for his grandson Simbolicus, who lived 16 years and 7 months.
EDCS-21900410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Simbolicus, detailing his age at death and identifying his grandfather, Publius Antelus Seleucus, as the dedicator.

EDCS-21900412.translation: Lucius Silius Dius made this for Silia Tycheni, his dearly loved and deserving mother, sacred to the spirits of the underworld.
EDCS-21900412.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Silius Dius to his mother, Silia Tycheni.

EDCS-21900413.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Soteri.
EDCS-21900413.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to Soteri.

EDCS-21900414.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Nicephor, a devoted father, made this for his most devoted daughter Doridi, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-21900414.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a father, Nicephor, to his daughter Doridi, noting her age at death.

EDCS-21900415.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sporidas made this for his dearest wife Statia Myrtis, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-21900415.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sporidas to his wife Statia Myrtis, stating her age at death.

EDCS-21900420.translation: Marcus Valerius Briccus carried out [this burial] as heir by will.
EDCS-21900420.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Valerius Briccus, as heir according to a will, arranged for the burial or monument.

EDCS-21900421.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Thermitana lived 3 years, 4 months, and 6 days. Valerius Eutyches and Lysidia Thaumasie, parents, made this for their most devoted daughter and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-21900421.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by parents Valerius Eutyches and Lysidia Thaumasie for their young daughter Valeria Thermitana and for themselves, likely indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-21900425.translation: [---]aviae Au[---] P(ublius?) Gemelius [---] and P(ublius) Geme[lius]
EDCS-21900425.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the names of funerary dedicators, possibly Publius Gemelius and another individual.

EDCS-21900435.translation: Avitianus the faithful rests here in the peace of Christ, who lived 12 years and rested on the 16th day before the Kalends of April in the fifteenth consulship of Theodosius and the second consulship of Flavius Valentinianus II.
EDCS-21900435.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Avitianus, recording his age, date of death, and the consuls of the year 439 AD.

EDCS-21900437.translation: For Crescentia, deserving in peace, who lived more or less 50 years, buried on the third day before the Kalends of December in the consulship of Flavius Opilio; her [family] provided for themselves during their lifetime.
EDCS-21900437.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crescentia, dating to the consulship of Flavius Opilio, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-21900438.translation: Here lies Iohannis, son of Tarric, who lived three years, two months, and six days, buried in peace on the seventeenth day before the Kalends of January in the 14th indiction.
EDCS-21900438.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iohannis, son of Tarric, giving his age and burial date, likely from the late Roman or Byzantine period.

EDCS-21900442.translation: Gaius Marcius Zoilus [dedicates this] to Minerva.
EDCS-21900442.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating something to the goddess Minerva by Gaius Marcius Zoilus.

EDCS-21900443.translation: Gnaeus Octavius, son of Aulus, a soldier of the Bolonian cohort, steward of Venus Tarichina, made the pavement and seats and took care of the repair of the temple.
EDCS-21900443.summary: This inscription records public works undertaken by Gnaeus Octavius, a soldier and steward, likely related to a temple of Venus Tarichina.

EDCS-21900445.translation: Here lies Adelfia, a most distinguished woman, provided for by Valerius the count.
EDCS-21900445.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Adelfia, a distinguished woman, placed by Valerius, a count.

EDCS-21900447.translation: The Syracusan people raised this statue to the most illustrious consular Roman, Perpennus, breathing again from his labors and seeing the dawn; therefore, the best men erected a statue, and she carries wisdom in her breast.
EDCS-21900447.summary: This is a laudatory inscription in Greek and Latin honoring the illustrious Roman official Perpennus, erected by the people of Syracuse.

EDCS-21900449.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iunius Alcibiades made [this] for Eros, freedman of Iunius Iulianus, proconsul's chamberlain, deserving in peace; he lived 20 years.
EDCS-21900449.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iunius Alcibiades for his deserving freedman Eros, who served as a chamberlain, dating to the Roman Empire.

EDCS-21900451.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Primus, Isidis's street sweeper, lived 80 years. Greetings, pious one.
EDCS-21900451.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Primus, a sweeper associated with the cult of Isis, dating to the Roman Empire.

EDCS-21900483.summary: This is a formal titulary inscription identifying Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus (Claudius).

EDCS-21900485.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Annius Anthimus, an excellent man. Hail, hail to you too.
EDCS-21900485.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annius Anthimus, described as an excellent man, from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-21900486.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Burnia Primula, daughter of Lucius, wife of Epaphroditus, freedman of the Emperors, lived for [X] years.
EDCS-21900486.summary: A funerary epitaph for Burnia Primula, recording her name, parentage, husband's status, and lifespan.

EDCS-21900487.translation: Lucius Caiat Argyr lived for [X] years.
EDCS-21900487.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Caiat Argyr, recording his name and lifespan.

EDCS-21900488.summary: An inscription recording the priestly and civic offices held by Cerialis.

EDCS-21900489.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Chrysi lived for [X] years. Felix, dutifully.
EDCS-21900489.summary: A funerary epitaph for Chrysi, recording her name and lifespan, with a dedication by Felix.

EDCS-21900490.translation: Cornelius Herm [erected this] for his wife Cornelia Epinice, with whom he lived for [X] years, and for their children.
EDCS-21900490.summary: A funerary monument set up by Cornelius Herm for his wife Cornelia Epinice and their children, mentioning their duration of marriage.

EDCS-21900491.translation: In memory of the master Macedo. By law, step back, friends, do not be sad, for we are all going to die.
EDCS-21900491.summary: A tombstone inscription for Macedo, serving as a philosophical reminder of mortality.

EDCS-21900492.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Euangelus lived for 9 years. Tychicus lived for 6 years. Thyche, mother, made [this] for her most dutiful sons.
EDCS-21900492.summary: A funerary epitaph for Euangelus and Tychicus, made by their mother Thyche, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-21900493.translation: Fulvius Nico lived for 50 years. Fulvius Ianuarius lived for 1 year and 3 months.
EDCS-21900493.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Fulvius Nico and Fulvius Ianuarius.

EDCS-21900494.translation: Iobacchus lived for 4 years and seven days.
EDCS-21900494.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iobacchus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-21900495.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cnaeus Julius Felix [erected this] for his son Cnaeus Julius Felix, who lived for 2 years, 9 months, and 1 day.
EDCS-21900495.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son named Cnaeus Julius Felix, set up by his father of the same name, recording the son's short lifespan.

EDCS-21900496.translation: Lucius Licinius Daphnus, freedman of Lucius, lived 35 years.
EDCS-21900496.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Lucius Licinius Daphnus.

EDCS-21900497.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning Marcus Lus Silvan.

EDCS-21900498.translation: Marcus Anllius Rufus, son of Spurius, of the Falerna tribe.
EDCS-21900498.summary: A fragmentary inscription identifying Marcus Anllius Rufus.

EDCS-21900499.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld; Anthe lived 7 years; his grandson made this for him.
EDCS-21900499.summary: A funerary inscription for Anthe, made by her grandson.

EDCS-21900508.translation: Sporus was laid to rest on the 3rd day before the Kalends of May; he lived 53 years. His devoted wife Constantia set this up for him because of his merit, with whom he lived for 12 years. He died in peace in the consulship of Constantius Augustus for the 8th time and Julian Caesar.
EDCS-21900508.summary: A funerary inscription for Sporus, detailing his life and death during the consulship of Constantius Augustus and Julian Caesar.

EDCS-21900509.translation: Silbana is laid to rest here, who rests in peace. She was laid to rest on the 11th day before the Kalends of December in the consulship of Flavius Bassus and Antiochus, most distinguished men.
EDCS-21900509.summary: A funerary inscription for Silbana, noting her repose and the consuls of the year.

EDCS-21900513.translation: Euplus is laid to rest here in peace, who lived five years.
EDCS-21900513.summary: A funerary inscription for Euplus, stating his age at death.

EDCS-21900516.translation: In memory of Christ, son of Kobbou.
EDCS-21900516.summary: A bilingual inscription in Latin and Greek, likely Christian, commemorating Christ, son of Kobbou.

EDCS-21900517.translation: A burial place in Syracuse, laid to rest on the 15th day before the Kalends of February, of Bernacles.
EDCS-21900517.summary: An inscription indicating a burial site in Syracuse and a date related to Bernacles.

EDCS-21900523.translation: Julius Innocentius lived in peace for 3 years, 8 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-21900523.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Julius Innocentius.

EDCS-21900527.translation: 9 months and 10 hours of the day. Innocen[tius?]
EDCS-21900527.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, possibly recording a period of time and a name.

EDCS-21900529.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Aepicaris made this for her very devoted and deserving freedman, Publius Aelius Eutychetes.
EDCS-21900529.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aelia Aepicaris for her freedman, Publius Aelius Eutychetes.

EDCS-21900530.translation: To Tiberius Flavius Diadumenus, freedman of the Augusti, his very devoted father, Flavia Victorina.
EDCS-21900530.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Flavia Victorina for her father, Tiberius Flavius Diadumenus.

EDCS-21900531.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Zethus lived for 6 years.
EDCS-21900531.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Zethus.

EDCS-21900532.translation: Sacred to the Emperors. Lucius Caelius Quadratus, son of Marcus, dedicated this at his own expense.
EDCS-21900532.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred monument by Lucius Caelius Quadratus, likely during imperial times.

EDCS-21900534.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Calbentius Motho lived for 40 years. Calbentia Fortunata made this for her brother.
EDCS-21900534.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Calbentius Motho and set up by his sister, Calbentia Fortunata.

EDCS-21900537.translation: Here rests in peace Placidia, unmarried, who lived for approximately 35 years, after the consulship of the most distinguished man Basilius, in the fourth indiction, in the 29th year.
EDCS-21900537.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Placidia, noting her lifespan, marital status, and dating the inscription to the 29th year of the 4th indiction, after Basilius' consulship.

EDCS-21900538.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly related to a location or a game, referencing Hercules.

EDCS-21900539.translation: To the memory of Vegelia, most unfortunate, who lived for 21 years, 3 months, and 17 days. Parthenopaeus made this for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-21900539.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Parthenopaeus for his wife Vegelia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-21900541.translation: For the prosperity of the times of our lords Constantius and Constantius, the best of emperors, Vitrasius Orfitus and Flavius Dulcitius, most excellent men and consuls of the province of Sicily, built this station from the ground up, at the urging of Flavius Valerianus, a ducenarius serving in the administration and prefect of the public courier service.
EDCS-21900541.summary: This inscription records the construction of a public station by two consuls of Sicily, initiated by an official serving in the cursus publicus, likely dating to the reign of Constantius II and possibly Constans (mid-4th century CE).

EDCS-22000787.translation: Ausanius the deacon lived in peace for 65 years, buried on the 7th day before the Ides of January.
EDCS-22000787.summary: This inscription records the burial date and age of Ausanius, a deacon, likely from a Christian context in late antiquity.

EDCS-22000788.translation: A place was granted by decree of the decurions to the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, pontifex maximus, in his third tribunician power, and consul for the third time.
EDCS-22000788.summary: This inscription records a public space being granted by decree to Emperor Hadrian, likely during his third consulship and third tribunician power (120-121 CE).

EDCS-22000789.translation: To Furia Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, wife of the Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus.
EDCS-22000789.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Furia Sabinia Tranquillina, the wife of Emperor Gordian III, likely dating to his reign (238-244 CE).

EDCS-22000792.translation: To Gaius Bultius Geminus Marcellus, a most distinguished youth, in honor of his father Geminius Ticianus, patron of the 12 tribes, a deserving patron.
EDCS-22000792.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Bultius Geminus Marcellus, a young man, and also mentions his father Geminius Ticianus, likely a local magistrate or benefactor in a city with tribal organization.

EDCS-22000793.translation: To Claudia Menti, sister of Flavius Mainus the senator, she lived for 3 years.
EDCS-22000793.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Menti, the young daughter of a senator, recording her short lifespan.

EDCS-22000794.translation: endowed with justice and singular sincerity, she surpassed all, Felicita.
EDCS-22000794.summary: This inscription praises an individual named Felicita for her virtues of justice and sincerity, likely a funerary or honorary epitaph.

EDCS-22000796.translation: To Lucius Acilius Rufus, son of Lucius, twice a magistrate of the colony of Thermitae.
EDCS-22000796.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Acilius Rufus, a local official who held the office of 'duovir' twice in the Roman colony of Thermitae.

EDCS-22000797.translation: To Lucius Amatio Maximus Memorianus, son of Lucius, Fabia tribe, quaestor of public funds, aedile, twice a magistrate, prefect to the Emperor Antoninus, the people of Lilybaeum granted this place publicly by decree of the decurions to a good citizen.
EDCS-22000797.summary: This inscription records the public grant of a place by the people of Lilybaeum to Lucius Amatio Maximus Memorianus, who held multiple local magistracies and a prefectural role under Emperor Antoninus Pius (138-161 CE).

EDCS-22000798.translation: To Marcus Rubellinus Cestianus Crassicianus, son of Publius, of the Publilia tribe, perpetual flamen of the deified Augusti, tribune (of the vigiles?).
EDCS-22000798.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Rubellinus Cestianus Crassicianus, who held religious office as a perpetual flamen and a military or civil position, likely in Rome.

EDCS-22000799.translation: Vitalis Appius and Nicolaus have granted this place to their excellent friend, by decree of the council of decurions.
EDCS-22000799.summary: This inscription records a grant of space made by Vitalis Appius and Nicolaus to a friend, decided by local officials.

EDCS-22000800.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Metilius Proculus made this for his most beloved wife, Claudia Sabina.
EDCS-22000800.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Metilius Proculus for his wife, Claudia Sabina.

EDCS-22000801.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Cornelius Glaphyrus made this for his freedman Caius Cornelius Philo, who deserved well.
EDCS-22000801.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a patron, Caius Cornelius Glaphyrus, for his deserving freedman, Caius Cornelius Philo.

EDCS-22000802.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Herennia Maurica.
EDCS-22000802.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Herennia Maurica.

EDCS-22000803.translation: To Marcus Marcellus. CIARIC BIO SEM.
EDCS-22000803.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Marcellus, with the meaning of the remaining words unclear.

EDCS-22000810.translation: By order of Marcus Albius Pollio, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, for the works of Caius Domitius Rufus, son of Caius, of the Voturia tribe, the craftsman SYMBVL dedicated this to Mercurius Augustus.
EDCS-22000810.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercurius Augustus by a craftsman, commissioned by Marcus Albius Pollio and related to the works of Caius Domitius Rufus.

EDCS-22000811.translation: While Plotinus and Rufa were safe, Logus, a slave and agent of the port of Lilybaeum, decorated this shrine in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-22000811.summary: This inscription records a shrine decorated by a slave agent of the port of Lilybaeum for his masters, Plotinus and Rufa, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-22000812.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, pontifex maximus, consul for the third time, imperator for the eighth time, with tribunician power for the twentieth time.
EDCS-22000812.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Tiberius Caesar, listing his titles and honors, likely from the early 1st century AD.

EDCS-22000814.translation: Dedicated on the seventh day before the Ides of September, when Emperor Antoninus Augustus was consul for the fourth time and Decius Caelius Balbinus for the second time, by Marcus Antius Gratillianus, quaestor pro praetore of the province of Sicily, a most distinguished man. To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, with tribunician power for the seventeenth time, consul for the third time, proconsul, father of the country, the colony of Helvia Augusta of the Lilybitani dedicated [this] devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-22000814.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument in Lilybaeum to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, dated to 210 AD, during his fourth consulship and Balbinus' second.

EDCS-22000815.translation: To our Emperor Caesar, our lord Flavius Valentinianus Pius Felix semper Augustus, Marcus Valerius Quintianus, a most distinguished man, consular governor of the province of Sicily, always devoted to his clemency and piety, [dedicated this] devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-22000815.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Valerius Quintianus, governor of Sicily, to Emperor Valentinian III, expressing devotion to his clemency and piety.

EDCS-22000816.translation: To the Emperor Caesar our lord Flavius Valens, Pious, Fortunate, ever Augustus, Marcus Valerius Quintianus, vir clarissimus, consular governor of the province of Sicily, forever devoted to his clemency and piety.
EDCS-22000816.summary: A dedication to Emperor Valens by Marcus Valerius Quintianus, consular governor of Sicily, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-22000819.translation: To Gaius Bultius Geminus Titianus, proconsul of the province of Sicily, most distinguished consul, for his outstanding goodwill towards the order and the fatherland, patron of the twelve tribes, who deserved it.
EDCS-22000819.summary: A dedication to Gaius Bultius Geminus Titianus, a former proconsul and consul, honored by the local tribes of Sicily for his benevolence, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-22000821.translation: Sosius Hyginianus, quaestor pro praetore of the province of Sicily, examined and publicly displayed the measures, from many.
EDCS-22000821.summary: An inscription recording that Sosius Hyginianus, quaestor pro praetore of Sicily, verified and published measurements, possibly related to a public works project.

EDCS-22000822.translation: The most distinguished order of the Augustan colony of Lilybaeum, out of its own funds, had this erected for Veturius Proculus Xprimo, quaestor pro praetore of the province of Sicily, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-22000822.summary: A public monument erected by the decurions of Lilybaeum for Veturius Proculus Xprimo, their quaestor pro praetore, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-22000823.translation: To Marcus Marcius Glaucus, son of Marcus Marcius and Claudia Bietis, father of a senator, honored with public equestrian rank, our patron, for his merits towards his citizens, a most distinguished man, admitted among the tribunes by the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, the tribules of the tribe of Jupiter Augustus, with their own funds, have granted this place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000823.summary: A dedication to Marcus Marcius Glaucus, honored by the tribules of Sicily for his public service and merits, likely during the reign of Commodus (177-192 CE).

EDCS-22000824.translation: From contributions collected, for a patron who deserved well of them, with Paconius Clodianus providing what was lacking from his own means.
EDCS-22000824.summary: A dedication using collected funds, with a supplementary contribution from Paconius Clodianus, for a patron who had served them well.

EDCS-22000828.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Hilara, who lived 27 years, 9 months, and 4 days. Publius Aelius Eutyches made this tomb for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-22000828.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelia Hilara, recording her age and commemorating her by her father, Publius Aelius Eutyches.

EDCS-22000829.translation: To Caius Curtius Threptus, son of Caius Curtius Threptus. The father granted this place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000829.summary: A funerary monument or grave plot granted by a father, Caius Curtius Threptus, to his son of the same name, by decree of the local council.

EDCS-22000830.summary: A brief dedication to a woman named Flavia, likely recognizing her good deeds or service.

EDCS-22000831.translation: To Pituania, daughter of Caius, of Vettulenus Quadratus, quaestor.
EDCS-22000831.summary: A dedication or mention of Pituania, daughter of Caius, connected to Vettulenus Quadratus, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-22000832.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Saturius lived for 7 years.
EDCS-22000832.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Saturius at age 7, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000833.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Publius Statius, Statius Quartus gave this place for himself, his wife Salvia, daughter of Caius, Felicula, and their descendants, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000833.summary: A funerary inscription recording the allocation of a burial plot by Statius Quartus for his family, designated by the local council, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000834.translation: O Publius Titinius, [dedicates?] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-22000834.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Titinius, date uncertain.

EDCS-22000839.summary: A dedication to the goddess Venus Erucina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000840.translation: Gave a gift to Venus Erucina.
EDCS-22000840.summary: A dedication recording a gift made to Venus Erucina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000841.translation: Sacred to Venus Erucina. I, Tinius, son of Publius, [dedicated this].
EDCS-22000841.summary: A dedication to Venus Erucina by Tinius, son of Publius, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000843.translation: Questor Pro Praetore and soldiers on Mount Erucus made [this]. Tribune of soldiers... Pr[...]
EDCS-22000843.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely related to military activity or a dedication on Mount Erucus, involving a quaestor pro praetore and soldiers, date uncertain.

EDCS-22000844.translation: Lucius Seius, son of Lucius, Aemilia Fir, at public expense funded [this].
EDCS-22000844.summary: An inscription recording a public works project funded by Lucius Seius and Aemilia Fir, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000846.summary: A very fragmentary inscription possibly containing a dedication to a goddess named Magna, by Lucius M., date uncertain.

EDCS-22000847.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Petronia Cyane set this up for herself while alive.
EDCS-22000847.summary: A funerary inscription recording Petronia Cyane setting up her own tomb in her lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22000850.translation: To Aelia Auge, my most virtuous wife. Publius Aelius Nigrinus, freedman of Augustus.
EDCS-22000850.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Auge, dedicated by her husband, Publius Aelius Nigrinus, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-22000851.translation: To Apolline. Lucius Carnius, son of Caius.
EDCS-22000851.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apolline, identified by her patron or owner, Lucius Carnius, son of Caius, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-22000853.translation: Marcus Ulpius Eutychus, freedman of Italicus, has set up an altar and base to Mercury, beyond the required amount, with his own money, in return for the office of a seviri, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000853.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar and base to Mercury by Marcus Ulpius Eutychus, a freedman, who paid for it from his own funds in exchange for the position of seviri, following a decree of the local council, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-22000854.translation: To the goddess Nemesis. Marcus Gellius Cad[--], from [--], D[--]
EDCS-22000854.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis, with partial text identifying the dedicator as Marcus Gellius Cad[--], likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-22000855.translation: Sextus Pompeius Mercator, one of the six lictors of the Augustales, has given this offering to Victory, beyond the required amount, with his own money, and has set it up.
EDCS-22000855.summary: This inscription records Sextus Pompeius Mercator, a member of the vigintiviri Augustales, dedicating an offering to Victory, funded by himself and set up by his decree, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-22000858.translation: To Iulia Augusta. By decree of the council of the people of Panhormus.
EDCS-22000858.summary: This inscription, dedicated to Iulia Augusta (likely Julia Domna), was erected by the state of Panhormus, and includes extensive imperial titulature for Septimius Severus, dating it to his reign (193-211 AD).

EDCS-22000859.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, son of Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus, father of the country, Augustus. By decree of the decurions of the people of Panhormus.
EDCS-22000859.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) by the state of Panhormus, with the consent of its decurions, dating to the co-rule of Septimius Severus and Caracalla (c. 198-211 AD).

EDCS-22000863.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely dating to his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-22000869.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar. The people of Panhormus, devoted to his divine power and majesty.
EDCS-22000869.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximinus (later Maximinus Daia) by the people of Panhormus, expressing devotion to his numen and maiestas, likely dating to his period as Caesar (c. 305-308 AD).

EDCS-22000872.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Herodianus, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Sicily, most rare judge, patron of the colony of Panhormus. The principal men, from bronze collected by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000872.summary: This inscription honors Tiberius Claudius Herodianus, a former governor of Sicily and patron of Panhormus, with a dedication erected by prominent citizens after a decree of the decurions, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-22000873.translation: To Quintus Aquilius Niger, proconsul, [the province of] Sicily, [of] Quintus Fabi Caesilius Titianus.
EDCS-22000873.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to the proconsul Quintus Aquilius Niger, likely by Quintus Fabi Caesilius Titianus.

EDCS-22000874.translation: To the departed spirits of Aurelius Rusticus, who served for 8 years in the century of Zeno, and Germanicianus, who served for 10 years; Valerius Victorinus, his heir, erected this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-22000874.summary: This funerary inscription honors two soldiers, Aurelius Rusticus and Germanicianus, with Valerius Victorinus as the heir.

EDCS-22000875.translation: To the departed spirits of Tacitius Iulianus, formerly an evocatus; Aurelia Lucilla, his dearest wife, and his son Iulius Tacitianus, his heirs, erected this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-22000875.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife and son for Tacitius Iulianus, who was formerly an evocatus.

EDCS-22000877.summary: This is a very short inscription identifying an individual named Primus as a triarch.

EDCS-22000878.translation: To Clodia Granilla, daughter of Sextus, [daughter] of Clodius Castus; Clodius Hermadion erected this for his most beloved daughter, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000878.summary: This inscription honors Clodia Granilla, daughter of Sextus and Clodius Castus, erected by Clodius Hermadion by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-22000879.translation: Hostilianus erected this at his own expense, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22000879.summary: This inscription records Hostilianus erecting a monument at his own expense, following a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-22000880.translation: To Paconius Clodianus, patron, for one who deserved well, from a collection of money.
EDCS-22000880.summary: This inscription honors the patron Paconius Clodianus, funded by a collection of money.

EDCS-22000884.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aebutius Vernus, a most rare example of a friend; Titus Aelius Vernus, his friend, [erected this].
EDCS-22000884.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the memory of Marcus Aebutius Vernus by his friend Titus Aelius Vernus.
