EDCS-65700762.translation: Slept in faith and peace, Proba, slave of God, ossuaria of the holy and catholic church of Lipara, departed this life in her 20th year, the day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-65700762.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Proba, a member of the church of Lipara, providing her age at death and date of passing.

EDCS-65700766.translation: Under the archons Skrebonius Nomentanus and Modestus.
EDCS-65700766.summary: This Greek inscription marks a date by the names of the officials Nomentanus and Modestus, likely in a civic context.

EDCS-66800019.translation: Publius Stertinius Threptus paved the Aelian road with his own money.
EDCS-66800019.summary: This Latin inscription records a public works project, the paving of the Aelian road, funded by Publius Stertinius Threptus.

EDCS-67400208.summary: This Latin inscription is a simple identification of Sphaerus as the slave of Nonus.

EDCS-67400212.translation: The memory of Benantius rests here in peace, who lived X years and was buried in the 9th indiction.
EDCS-67400212.summary: This Latin funerary inscription records the burial of Benantius, mentioning his lifespan and the date of his deposition by indiction.

EDCS-67400264.translation: Severus, honest and blameless, lived 35 years, 3 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-67400264.summary: This Greek funerary inscription records the lifespan of Severus, noting his virtues and precise age at death.

EDCS-68100075.translation: Lucius Quinctius, son of Caius, approved.
EDCS-68100075.summary: This Latin inscription records the approval of an action or document by Lucius Quinctius, son of Caius, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-68100076.translation: Lucius Quinctius, son of Caius, approved.
EDCS-68100076.summary: This Latin inscription records the approval of an action or document by Lucius Quinctius, son of Caius, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-68100077.translation: Gaius Paperios, son of Tiberius, and Marcus Populicios, son of Lucius, quaestors, approved [this].
EDCS-68100077.summary: This inscription records the approval of an action by two quaestors, Gaius Paperios and Marcus Populicios.

EDCS-68100078.translation: Gaius Sestius, son of Publius, Quintus Salonius, son of Quintus, and Sextus Viroen approved [this].
EDCS-68100078.summary: This inscription records the approval of an action by three individuals: Gaius Sestius, Quintus Salonius, and Sextus Viroen.

EDCS-68300024.summary: This is a possessive or dedicatory inscription referring to Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus.

EDCS-68800059.translation: I vowed [this] for Lucius, freedman, aged 4.
EDCS-68800059.summary: This is a vow made on behalf of a freedman named Lucius who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-68800060.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pardus the murmillo lived 30 years. Sergia Gaia made this for herself at her own expense.
EDCS-68800060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pardus, a murmillo gladiator who lived to 30, funded by Sergia Gaia.

EDCS-68800064.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Fausta.

EDCS-68800066.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to C.C.I. Africanus, possibly as a builder or commissioner of a monument.

EDCS-69000037.translation: Under the hierapolos Diogenes, in the month of Apellaios, on the eleventh day of the waxing moon, Clenagoras, son of Ariston, bought a house from Agathocles for four talents. Witnesses: Lissos, son of Tharsynon; Iaron, son of Agatharchos; Timon, son of Xenon; Dionysius, son of Philinus; Hipposthenes, son of Praton.
EDCS-69000037.summary: This inscription is a deed recording the purchase of a house in the month of Apellaios by Clenagoras from Agathocles, with named witnesses and officials.

EDCS-69000042.translation: Under [consul?] Ch. L. [name], the buyer [name], son of Hegelos, bought a house with a portico in the street of Heracles, and the courtyards from the bedroom, along with the well and the mill, a private [property?], and the door frames, for five talents from Dikaiagora, daughter of Antallos. Tenth [part?] first, sixth second, sixth second, sixth second, tenth second, sixth second or tenth first, twelfth, sixth second, third, and tenth. [Purchased from?] Prastellidas, E.].
EDCS-69000042.summary: This is a fragmentary deed recording the purchase of a house, its features, and appurtenances by an unnamed buyer from Dikaiagora, with complex details about fractional payments and witnesses, likely from the Roman period given the mix of Greek and Latin elements.

EDCS-69200085.translation: To all the gods. The soldiers who served by ship, the Halaisinoi, Kalaktinoi, Herbitaioi, Amistratinoi, [dedicated this] to Caninius Niger out of goodwill.
EDCS-69200085.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by soldiers from various communities serving by ship to Caninius Niger, expressing their goodwill.

EDCS-70100015.translation: In the Macillian arcades, four. And from the consular [buildings?].
EDCS-70100015.summary: This inscription appears to be a building or property marker, possibly indicating locations or divisions within the Macillian arcades and consular buildings, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-70200157.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely names individuals or a group from Cossura, possibly an ethnic or a family name, with its dating and precise context unknown due to damage.

EDCS-70700178.translation: Pithacos, son of Aphēstion, Saetas, son of Euryphilus, Matylas, son of Calles, Thopyllos, son of Xenon, Athanis, son of Antandros, Charon, son of Hagesarchos, Silanos, son of Hagesarchos, Xenon, son of Hīnys, Hagiā, daughter of Philodamos, Deinīā, daughter of Sphīras, Philon, son of Naqois.
EDCS-70700178.summary: This is a list of names, likely a funerary epigram or a list of members of a family or association, probably from a Greek-speaking region within the Roman Empire.

EDCS-70800128.translation: The eloquent speech of Eukles, who inscribed it, shall benefit no one regarding Mestor. The eloquent speech of Simia, daughter of Mikythos, shall benefit no one regarding Mestor. The eloquent speech of Pithāqos, son of Loos, shall benefit no one regarding Mestor. The eloquent speech of [...], son of RILIpou, shall benefit no one regarding Mestor. Philondas, son of Khoirinas, and his glory shall benefit no one regarding Mestor. The eloquent speech of Mestor, son of Eikelos, and all the eloquent speeches of Eikelos shall benefit no one regarding Mestor. The archistrategos (general) of Aischinas, in place of whom he owed speeches to Mestor.
EDCS-70800128.summary: This is a complex legal or ritualistic inscription, likely from a Greek-speaking context, detailing a curse or a declaration that certain 'eloquent speeches' or legal claims will not benefit specified individuals, particularly concerning someone named Mestor, possibly dating to the Hellenistic or Roman imperial period.

EDCS-70800250.translation: Pyrrhos, and anything for Artamon, nor for Eurios, as per Artamon.
EDCS-70800250.summary: This inscription seems to be a legal or contractual statement, possibly related to a dispute or agreement involving individuals named Pyrrhos, Artamon, and Eurios, likely from a Greek-speaking area in the Roman period.

EDCS-70800251.translation: Pratōtas, Olympias, Deinīā, Kibos, Charilos, Menyllos, Mitiadas, Deinīā, Phileiā, Teletas, Histiarchos, Andriscos, Philainos, Deinīis, Atopphas.
EDCS-70800251.summary: This is a list of Greek personal names, possibly constituting a funerary dedication or a record of members of a group, found in a Roman provincial context.

EDCS-70800252.translation: Archylis, Zilias, Sosistratos, and whoever else intends or says or does anything for them, have agreed upon useful [things] for Selinon.
EDCS-70800252.summary: This inscription records an agreement or a legal settlement concerning individuals named Archylis, Zilias, and Sosistratos, relating to 'useful things' for Selinon, likely from a Greek-speaking community in the Roman era.

EDCS-70800259.translation: Ainesidamos, son of Pasillos, (one of the) Hekendekatas.
EDCS-70800259.summary: This inscription records the name of an individual, Ainesidamos, son of Pasillos, and identifies him as belonging to a group called the Hekendekatas, likely from a Greek-speaking region in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-70800260.summary: This inscription identifies an individual named Xenon and states his origin as Korkyra (Corfu), likely a simple identification or gravestone, from the Roman period.

EDCS-70800261.translation: Cheilinos, son of Simos, the eighth.
EDCS-70800261.summary: This inscription names an individual, Cheilinos, son of Simos, and identifies him as 'the eighth', possibly indicating a sequence, rank, or a specific group, from a Greek-speaking context in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-70800263.summary: This is a funerary epigram or epitaph, likely from a tombstone, indicating a person named Archeas and their position or group within a structure or society.

EDCS-70800264.translation: Xenocrates, first of the second phratry.
EDCS-70800264.summary: This inscription likely refers to a member of a brotherhood or guild, identifying Xenocrates by name and his rank within the second phratry.

EDCS-70800266.summary: This is a patronymic inscription, identifying Eraton as the son of Straton, possibly on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-70800268.translation: [...]us, of Eukleidas, died on the fourteenth.
EDCS-70800268.summary: This inscription records the death of a person, possibly named Eukleidas or related to him, with a specific date indicated as the fourteenth, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-70800269.translation: Nicodamus, of Parmenides, on the fourteenth.
EDCS-70800269.summary: This inscription records Nicodamus, son or associated with Parmenides, and a date of death or event on the fourteenth, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-70800271.translation: Xenos, son of Exakios, of the eleventh phratry.
EDCS-70800271.summary: This inscription identifies Xenos, son of Exakios, and his affiliation with the eleventh phratry, likely for civic or religious purposes.

EDCS-70800365.summary: This is a short dedication or identification, where Belios states his name and identity as Hierraros, possibly a title or epithet.

EDCS-70900013.translation: In the priesthood of Dionysius, son of Sopatros, during the hieromnamon-ship of Artemidoros, son of Dossios the Gradanian, the double gate, the men's dining rooms, the front part were roofed, [in honor of...] [---].
EDCS-70900013.summary: This is a dedication for a public building project, recording the officials during whose tenure the roof of a double gate and men's dining rooms was completed, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-70900018.summary: This brief inscription identifies a person named Archero and their role as a 'mnamon', possibly an official or record-keeper.

EDCS-70900052.translation: Ar. Clenias, the beautiful, son of Pydios.
EDCS-70900052.summary: This inscription is likely a dedication or epitaph, praising Clenias, son of Pydios, possibly for his beauty or noble qualities.

EDCS-70900059.translation: Euthea, daughter of Cleon, and Agathonymus, son of Sylinus, the beautiful, and Simon. Greetings.
EDCS-70900059.summary: A funerary monument for Euthea, Agathonymus, and Simon, with names listed in Greek.

EDCS-70900060.translation: To Hephaestus, from Ariston, Xenon, Emmenidas, Xenios, Synon, Eumnastos, Enormos. Greetings.
EDCS-70900060.summary: A dedication to Hephaestus by several individuals, listing their Greek names.

EDCS-70900062.translation: A pathway, as it seemed to you, Thestos, from Phriondas and Hydas, to Empedion. Greetings.
EDCS-70900062.summary: An epitaph or dedication for Empedion, mentioning Phriondas and Hydas and the Greek name Thestos, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900066.translation: A memorial for Heracleidas, son of Amn[---].
EDCS-70900066.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Heracleidas, son of Amn[---], identified by his Greek name.

EDCS-70900393.translation: Ruski Magne, greetings. You lived honorably.
EDCS-70900393.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ruski Magne, praising his honorable life, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900394.summary: An inscription identifying Damatios, son of Mamarkos, possibly a funerary or ownership marker.

EDCS-70900614.translation: To Sa, from Saïskos, for Polyxenos.
EDCS-70900614.summary: A dedication or funerary marker from Saïskos to Polyxenos, featuring Greek names.

EDCS-70900618.summary: An inscription referring to something belonging to Adeonzinus and described as 'public', possibly related to civic property or a public monument.

EDCS-70900624.translation: Deiphobus the Stilanaian dedicated me to you, City, as it seemed fitting to you.
EDCS-70900624.summary: A dedication by Deiphobus of Stilana to the City, indicating a civic dedication, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900625.translation: For Beieuthion, two pounds, by Mathaios.
EDCS-70900625.summary: A record of payment or a dedication of two pounds by Mathaios to Beieuthion, potentially related to a funerary context or a business transaction.

EDCS-70900626.translation: Phidias dedicates that Sosis and Epidamos and Persephassa, daughter of Pasikrates, may prosper, and a feast of sacrificial meat from all around the trench, according to the judgment that the god brings.
EDCS-70900626.summary: This is a dedication concerning the prosperity of certain individuals and a sacrificial feast, possibly related to a cultic practice or ritual.

EDCS-70900627.translation: Klitandra dedicated to Nemesion three petramina as first fruits.
EDCS-70900627.summary: This is a dedication by Klitandra to Nemesion, offering a specific quantity of an unknown item ('petramina') as first fruits.

EDCS-70900628.translation: Pandosia 'pigizei' and Hyko 'pigizei' Rapa and Nido 'pigizei' Ninini.
EDCS-70900628.summary: This inscription lists several female names followed by the verb 'pigizei', possibly indicating a ritual or social group affiliation.

EDCS-70900629.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or dedication-related note, mentioning the recipient Lecho and the name Dion, followed by an unclear term 'psolai'.

EDCS-70900631.summary: This is a simple maker's signature, likely found on a work of art or a manufactured object.

EDCS-70900657.translation: Sosippos bought from Philoxenos, son of Silanos, a house and all the furnishings within the house for one hundred talents and five litrai, during the priesthood of Epidamagogos, son of Damaretes, and the archonship of Assinos, son of Outos.
EDCS-70900657.summary: This is a record of a property sale, including the price and the officials (priest and archon) during whose tenure the transaction occurred.

EDCS-70900693.translation: First tribe, phratria of Ripa, Orthos, son of Emmenidas.
EDCS-70900693.summary: This inscription records the classification of an individual within a tribal and phratric structure, identifying Orthos, son of Emmenidas.

EDCS-70900694.translation: Second tribe, phratria of Ripa, Sosis, son of Karaios.
EDCS-70900694.summary: This inscription records the classification of an individual within a tribal and phratric structure, identifying Sosis, son of Karaios.

EDCS-70900695.translation: Third tribe, phratria of Katel, Kephalos, son of Rakaikos.
EDCS-70900695.summary: This inscription records the classification of an individual within a tribal and phratric structure, identifying Kephalos, son of Rakaikos.

EDCS-70900696.translation: Second tribe, phratria of Ple, Kantharos, son of Agelochos.
EDCS-70900696.summary: This inscription records the classification of an individual within a tribal and phratric structure, identifying Kantharos, son of Agelochos.

EDCS-70900711.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating ownership or origin from a person named Kallikrates.

EDCS-70900712.translation: To Adranos, during the priesthood of Theudoros and Philon, the sacrificers Nicasion son of Eupolemos, Nemes, Herakleidas, Agathon son of Eudamos.
EDCS-70900712.summary: This inscription lists names, including priests and sacrificers, likely dedicating something to the god Adranos.

EDCS-70900713.translation: Deutera, phratry of Ple, Phintylos son of Pheiridios.
EDCS-70900713.summary: This inscription records tribal and phratry affiliation, along with an individual's name.

EDCS-70900723.summary: This inscription records the name Nikolaos and his role as a phylax.

EDCS-70900724.translation: You, shameless sodomite, Onotor, son of Kallimades.
EDCS-70900724.summary: This inscription is an insult directed at Onotor, son of Kallimades.

EDCS-70900725.translation: Nymphodoros used to bring the pleasing fish-seller. Damylis, the pleasing wife of Nymphodoros.
EDCS-70900725.summary: This inscription appears to be a somewhat garbled or humorous comment about a fish-seller and a woman named Damylis.

EDCS-70900740.translation: For the month Proti balios, during the priesthood of Pithon, Alkipos son of Pyrrhichos bought a house tax-free from Onos son of Alkippos. Ambokhos, Mothon, Kentos, for the honor, three staters of silver.
EDCS-70900740.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a house tax-free, detailing the buyer, seller, and payment, likely associated with a religious or communal fund.

EDCS-70900744.summary: This is a simple funerary or greeting inscription for a woman named Frone.

EDCS-70900745.translation: To Artemos son of Charillos, greetings.
EDCS-70900745.summary: This is a dedication or greeting inscription addressed to Artemos son of Charillos.

EDCS-70900750.summary: This is a simple greeting inscription addressed to Asklepiades.

EDCS-70900755.summary: This is a simple greeting or epitaph for a person named Zotikos.

EDCS-70900756.translation: During the priesthood of Apollonius, Theodoros, son of Apollodoros, most powerful, made a vow.
EDCS-70900756.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Theodoros during the priesthood of Apollonius.

EDCS-70900757.translation: Molossos, son of Hiketas, buys land from Imeraios for one hundred talents for Men of the Street. Ambokhos, son of Xenotimos, son of Herakleidas.
EDCS-70900757.summary: This inscription records a land sale concerning land dedicated to Men of the Street.

EDCS-70900783.translation: Having served as gymnasium director, Euboulidas, son of Herakleios.
EDCS-70900783.summary: This inscription honors Euboulidas, son of Herakleios, for his service as gymnasium director.

EDCS-70900791.translation: I am Smordos, son of Smordos.
EDCS-70900791.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Smordos, son of Smordos.

EDCS-70900796.summary: This is a short funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Stratonos.

EDCS-70900798.translation: Pyrrhinos, son of Pythikkas, made this.
EDCS-70900798.summary: This inscription records that Pyrrhinos, son of Pythikkas, was the maker of the object.

EDCS-70900799.translation: Honoring prudence and revering justice, Hippe, in the prime of life, left the spirit of her life.
EDCS-70900799.summary: This is a poetic epitaph for Hippe, praising her virtues of prudence and justice.

EDCS-70900802.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Aphrodite by Aristokleia.

EDCS-70900806.translation: Antandros, son of Pythios, dedicated the tenth to Apollo Patroos.
EDCS-70900806.summary: This inscription records a tenth offering dedicated to Apollo Patroos by Antandros, son of Pythios.

EDCS-70900815.translation: Aischrion and Nymphodoros, sons of Bombylinos.
EDCS-70900815.summary: This is a funerary epitaph naming two individuals, Aischrion and Nymphodoros.

EDCS-70900816.translation: Tertis lies here, having lived 4 years, 6 months, and 5 days. Tertis and Phoebe made this for their sweetest child.
EDCS-70900816.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of a child named Tertis, erected by his parents.

EDCS-70900827.translation: Agemachos, son of Polyarchos, for Eloris and Timassai.
EDCS-70900827.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Agemachos to Eloris and Timassai.

EDCS-70900828.translation: During the gymnasiarchy of Onasikrates, son of Onasikrates, the young men who sponsored Agatharchidas, son of Zoilus, son of Nymphon, son of Artamon, son of Archonidas...
EDCS-70900828.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription related to a gymnasiarchy and likely sponsoring the career of Agatharchidas.

EDCS-70900858.summary: This is a very short inscription likely referring to the maker of an object or monument.

EDCS-70900860.translation: Cleodemos, son of Daimachos; Deinomenes, son of Ptelas; Anaxaretas, son of Mega; Arescous, son of Archebolas; Hippias.
EDCS-70900860.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly of officials, donors, or members of a group.

EDCS-70900862.translation: Nicomedes, son of Parmenios, in the time of the dead.
EDCS-70900862.summary: This inscription likely refers to Nicomedes in the context of funerary rites or a memorial.

EDCS-70900866.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Damanetus and his father Mnasias.

EDCS-70900958.translation: Deutera, tribe, phratry, Dyn..., Adron, son of Herakleides.
EDCS-70900958.summary: This inscription appears to record tribal and phratry affiliations along with personal names.

EDCS-70900965.translation: Dion, son of Dai, from Akragas.
EDCS-70900965.summary: This inscription identifies Dion, son of Dai, as being from Akragas.

EDCS-70900966.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Artemis.

EDCS-70900972.translation: Pyrria, daughter of Samonidas, a statue.
EDCS-70900972.summary: This inscription records a statue dedicated by Pyrria.

EDCS-70900983.summary: This inscription refers to a garment belonging to Antallos.

EDCS-70900988.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Elaielinus.

EDCS-70901031.translation: Marcia, wife, [may you be] good and blameless; greetings.
EDCS-70901031.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcia, praising her virtues.

EDCS-70901041.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Agatharchos.

EDCS-70901043.translation: Demetrios, son of Kephisodoros, of Athens.
EDCS-70901043.summary: This inscription identifies Demetrios of Athens, son of Kephisodoros.

EDCS-70901050.summary: This inscription may be a dedication or statement of ownership, possibly related to the deity Damosion.

EDCS-70901077.translation: To Artemois, daughter of Artemidotos; greetings.
EDCS-70901077.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory greeting to Artemois.

EDCS-71000055.translation: Leptinas, Kephalos, son of Hyo, son of Hogdoas.
EDCS-71000055.summary: This inscription records a patronymic and possibly a lineage for Leptinas, Kephalos, and their ancestors.

EDCS-71000056.summary: This is a personal name, likely indicating an owner or dedicator.

EDCS-71000068.summary: This inscription records a personal name, likely an owner or dedicator.

EDCS-71000071.translation: Pyrrhia, daughter of Ops, sister of the phratry
EDCS-71000071.summary: This inscription records a woman's name and her familial and fraternity relationships.

EDCS-71000079.translation: Dikaiarchos, son of Hippias, brother of Hekta's phratry, brother of Hekta's phratry
EDCS-71000079.summary: This inscription records a man's name and his fraternity affiliations.

EDCS-71000082.translation: Chromios, son of Gyga, third from the last
EDCS-71000082.summary: This inscription records a personal name and a positional designation, possibly in a list or sequence.

EDCS-71000085.summary: This inscription records a personal name, likely an owner or dedicator.

EDCS-71000087.translation: Clearis tos, son of Ka'iqos, tenth
EDCS-71000087.summary: This inscription records a personal name and a numerical designation, possibly indicating a rank or position.

EDCS-71000090.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing personal names.

EDCS-71000091.translation: Paidi kos, son of Thya, brother of the neatas phratry
EDCS-71000091.summary: This inscription records a man's name and his fraternity affiliation.

EDCS-71000093.summary: This inscription records a personal name and a numerical designation, possibly indicating a rank or position.

EDCS-71000102.translation: Aratos, leader of the thirteenth phratry.
EDCS-71000102.summary: This inscription records Aratos as the leader of the thirteenth phratry, likely a civic or religious association.

EDCS-71000103.translation: Thripainos, son of Antigonos, Thripainos, son of Hektas, son of Antigonos.
EDCS-71000103.summary: This inscription appears to be a patronymic listing for Thripainos, indicating his lineage.

EDCS-71000117.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or naming formula, identifying Glaukias as the son of Eukletas.

EDCS-71000119.summary: This inscription names Eucharmos, son of Henatas, likely a personal dedication or identification.

EDCS-71000120.translation: Dion, son of Nemonaios, of the phratry of Henatas; thirteenth phratry of the Thirtieth; Henatas, phratry leader, ten litrai.
EDCS-71000120.summary: This inscription details membership and potentially a financial contribution (ten litrai) within specific phratries for Dion and Henatas.

EDCS-71000121.translation: Faullos, from the phratry of Henatas, son of Matrodoros.
EDCS-71000121.summary: This inscription identifies Faullos as a member of the phratry of Henatas and son of Matrodoros.

EDCS-71000122.translation: Hipparinos, son of Saio, leader of the tenth.
EDCS-71000122.summary: This inscription records Hipparinos, son of Saio, as the leader of the tenth group or phratry.

EDCS-71000123.summary: This inscription names Herakleidas, son of Euormos, possibly recording a quantity or a designation with the number eight.

EDCS-71000124.translation: Eukles, son of Hermaios, a deceliterary offering of the fourteenth.
EDCS-71000124.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a 'deceliterary' (ten litrai) item by Eukles, son of Hermaios, associated with the fourteenth group.

EDCS-71000125.translation: Thearidas, son of Podas, from the 'Nethas'.
EDCS-71000125.summary: This inscription identifies Thearidas, son of Podas, and indicates he is from a place or group called 'Nethas'.

EDCS-71000128.summary: This inscription likely records a personal account or debt, listing Zeuxias and a quantity related to Euopis.

EDCS-71000131.summary: This inscription is a simple patronymic, identifying Hipparchos, son of Athanis.

EDCS-71000132.translation: Sicanos, son of Apellion, the fourteenth.
EDCS-71000132.summary: This inscription possibly names Sicanos, son of Apellion, and indicates his rank or position as fourteenth.

EDCS-71000134.translation: For Pyri, of Simos, phratry of Hectas.
EDCS-71000134.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or record related to Pyri, associated with Simos and the phratry of Hectas.

EDCS-71000139.translation: Theon, son of Nikias, the fourth from the youngest, the fourteenth, 'fourth', 'tenth'.
EDCS-71000139.summary: This inscription identifies Theon, son of Nikias, and seems to denote his rank or order within a group, possibly related to age and a specific division.

EDCS-71000141.summary: This fragmentary inscription refers to someone or something belonging to Suchaios and a phratry.

EDCS-71000143.summary: This inscription is a patronymic, identifying Taurēas, son of Theodoros.

EDCS-71000144.translation: Sakys, son of Kalas, son of Polykles, son of Kalas.
EDCS-71000144.summary: This inscription records a genealogy, listing Sakys as the son of Kalas, who was the son of Polykles, who was also the son of Kalas.

EDCS-71000148.translation: Sostratos, son of X..., the fourteenth.
EDCS-71000148.summary: This inscription identifies Sostratos, son of X, and indicates his position as fourteenth, possibly in a sequence or rank.

EDCS-71000153.translation: Euklētas, son of Asaros, phratry of Hectas.
EDCS-71000153.summary: This inscription identifies Euklētas, son of Asaros, and places him within the phratry of Hectas.

EDCS-71000154.translation: Mazas, Sosia, Timon, the twelfth of Tetartos, brotherhood.
EDCS-71000154.summary: This inscription records a membership list of a brotherhood, likely a funerary or social association, with names and a numerical designation.

EDCS-71000155.translation: of Los, of Deinomachus, the fourteenth.
EDCS-71000155.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment listing names and a numerical designation, possibly related to membership or ranking within a group.

EDCS-71000159.translation: of Nytos, of Theognetos, brotherhood, fourth, last.
EDCS-71000159.summary: This inscription lists names and describes a position within a brotherhood as 'fourth' and 'last', indicating a hierarchical structure.

EDCS-71000161.translation: Henatas, Aristion, Deinion, Timon, Philinos, Henatas.
EDCS-71000161.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly members of a group or family, with repetitions that might indicate lineage or affiliation.

EDCS-71000164.summary: This inscription is a simple repetition of two names, Glykinos and Simias, possibly indicating a couple or paired individuals.

EDCS-71000167.summary: This inscription records names and a numerical designation 'fifth', likely referring to a rank or position within a group or order.

EDCS-71000174.translation: of Osis, of Thrasuos, Henatas, the third of the thirty.
EDCS-71000174.summary: This inscription names individuals and indicates a position as 'third of the thirty', suggesting a structured group or council.

EDCS-71000175.translation: Zoilus, son of Polemarchos, of the twelfth brotherhood.
EDCS-71000175.summary: This inscription records an individual, Zoilus, son of Polemarchos, and his membership in the twelfth brotherhood.

EDCS-71000176.translation: Euangelos, son of Simos, of the eighth brotherhood.
EDCS-71000176.summary: This inscription identifies Euangelos, son of Simos, and his affiliation with the eighth brotherhood.

EDCS-71000177.translation: Thearidas, son of Brachyllos, of the Henatas.
EDCS-71000177.summary: This inscription records the names Thearidas and Brachyllos, possibly indicating a father-son relationship and affiliation with a group called Henatas.

EDCS-71000178.translation: Sikanos, son of Archonidas, neetos (a measure of grain).
EDCS-71000178.summary: This inscription records a measure of grain, possibly related to tribute or taxation.

EDCS-71000179.translation: Daikrates, son of Lykiskos, phratra (brotherhood) of the fifth.
EDCS-71000179.summary: This inscription likely records membership in a phratry or brotherhood, possibly with a designation of fifth rank or division.

EDCS-71000180.translation: Aischylos, son of Aristios, fourth of the tenth.
EDCS-71000180.summary: This inscription may refer to a rank, division, or office held by Aischylos, son of Aristios.

EDCS-71000181.translation: Silanos, son of Ithagenes, fourth of the tenth.
EDCS-71000181.summary: This inscription likely denotes a position or rank, possibly military or administrative, held by Silanos, son of Ithagenes.

EDCS-71000182.translation: Antisthenes, son of Parmenon, phratra (brotherhood) of the fourteenth.
EDCS-71000182.summary: This inscription records Antisthenes, son of Parmenon, as belonging to the fourteenth phratry or brotherhood.

EDCS-71000183.summary: This inscription indicates Herakleidas, son of Archonidas, held a position or rank designated as 'fourth'.

EDCS-71000184.translation: Herakleidas, son of Kratinos, fifth of the fifth.
EDCS-71000184.summary: This inscription likely refers to a hierarchical position or division within a group, held by Herakleidas, son of Kratinos.

EDCS-71000185.translation: Herakleios, son of Theston, Henatas (a tribal division or group).
EDCS-71000185.summary: This inscription records Herakleios, son of Theston, belonging to the Henatas group or division.

EDCS-71000186.summary: This inscription marks Pyrrichos, son of Nikophanes, with the designation 'eighth', possibly indicating rank or order.

EDCS-71000188.translation: Tain Keas Thrasys, son of Emmenidas, with his spear a brave warrior for all, he is the highest unblemished.
EDCS-71000188.summary: This funerary epigram praises Thrasys, son of Emmenidas, as a valiant and exemplary warrior, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71000189.translation: Hestia, fifth, first, granted one talent of silver, interest-free, to Hieron. Sosandros, son of Sosios, twelfth, second. Nemenios, ninth, second. Simias.
EDCS-71000189.summary: This inscription records a financial transaction where Hestia granted a talent of silver interest-free to Hieron, with several individuals named as parties or guarantors.

EDCS-71000198.translation: Under Lykos, son of Meniskos, on the eighth, the twenty-eighth [day?], Damatrios, the second to last day, sold the goods as first pledge, to Xeni, daughter of Kr...nida, twelfth, second. All the pottery near the shrine and the surrounding area, two talents, according to the law. Ambokhos, son of Sosistratos, son of Theon, seventh, second.
EDCS-71000198.summary: This inscription details a sale of pottery and surrounding land by Damatrios to Xeni, with Ambokhos acting as guarantor, specifying the transaction was conducted under Lykos and Xeni, likely in the 2nd century BCE.

EDCS-71000200.translation: Under Heraion, on the sixth of the current month, the directors of the market: Dinarkhos, son of Kleandros; Sosistratos, son of Theon, first of the ninth [class]. Bought the house and all of Dion's shop and the doorposts, shared walls, next to Philoxenos and Thrasyllos, in the lane above Gaos and Phersophassa, from Dion, son of Herakleidas, first of the fourth [class], forty talents. Ambokhoi: Aristons, son of Emmenidas, first of the ninth [class]; Philippos, son of Pausanias, first of the ninth [class]; Artamidôros, son of Herakleidas, first of the fourth [class]; Pausanias, son of Sosikrates, first of the ninth [class]; Herakleios, son of Nikôn, first of the third [class]; Sannôn, son of Zopyros, first of the fourth [class]; Simos, son of Gelôios, first of the ninth [class]; Nikôn, son of Euthymenes, first of the sixth [class]; Theôdoros, son of Damon, first of the sixth [class]; Gelôn, son of Kallistratos, first of the sixth [class].
EDCS-71000200.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a house and shop by Sosistratos and Dinarkhos from Dion, with a lengthy list of guarantors (Ambokhoi) and officials named, dated to the 2nd century BCE.

EDCS-71000201.translation: Two talents. Ambokhoi: Euthykles, son of Eudoxos, second, third, first. Nikis, son of Stratôn, ninth, second. Two [talents?]. First. Sokrates, son of Sosikrates, of Herakleidas, tenth, first. Leonis, of Gnathon, fifth, first. Theotimos, son of Lamiskos, ninth, first. Koragos, son of Philagros, of Ainêtos, Nikaretos.
EDCS-71000201.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, primarily identified as 'Ambokhoi' (guarantors) for a financial transaction involving two talents, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000202.summary: A very short inscription, likely an ownership mark or a simple dedication, naming Theodosios, son of Hippias.

EDCS-71000203.translation: Under Archagathos, son of Gelôios, on the sixth, the tenth [day?], Mnasis, daughter of Dion, fifth, first. Bought the 'gyan' [a type of object, possibly a vessel] with three discus, the width, and the length from the stream of Konghokranaios, up to the straps, the 'kullas' [possibly a type of jar or vessel], from Athanis, son of Basias, one hundred talents. Ambokhoi: Theudosios, son of Theudôrou, second of the sixth [class]; Theotimos, son of Lamiskos; Ameinôn, son of Damatrios; Nymphôdoros, son of Damon; Theôdoros, son of Aristogenes; Artamidôros, son of Theodosiou; Myskôn, son of Andrônos; Protoxos, son of Sosias; Philon, son of Ainesidamos; Orthôn, son of Philodamos.
EDCS-71000203.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a specific object (gyan) and its dimensions from Athanis by Mnasis, with a substantial sum of one hundred talents, and a list of guarantors, likely dating to the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000205.translation: Under Philinos, son of Dionysios, on the fourth of the current month, the directors of the market: Pausanias, son of Philistion; Herakleidas, son of Euthykles. Bought half of the plot of land in Tyrakinaias, which belonged to Nymphôdoros, and all that was within, common and undivided, from Sosigenes and Ariston, two hundred and fifty talents, from Sosigenes, son of Apollonidas, and Ariston, son of Zoilus. Ambokhoi: Krêton, son of Olympios; Apollônios, son of Phintias; Protoxos, son of Lykiskos; Timokrates, son of Sophronidas; [son of] Zoilus; Philarkhos, son of Antallos; [son of] Andrea; Agesandros, son of Damarkhos; [son of] Sannôn, son of Philon; [son of] Istieios.
EDCS-71000205.summary: This inscription records the purchase of half a plot of land and its contents by Pausanias and Herakleidas from Sosigenes and Ariston for a significant sum, with numerous guarantors listed, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000208.translation: Under Ch... onei... archos, son of Hagelokhos, sixth, second. Bought the house with a portico, the lane of Herakles, and the courtyards from the thalamus, and the well, and the mill, private, and the five doorposts, for five talents, from Dikaiagoras, daughter of Antallos, tenth, first. Sixth, second. Sixth, second. Sixth, second. Tenth, second. Sixth, second or tenth, first. Twelfth. Sixth, second. Third and tenth. [part of a name]nasis, [part of a name]enês, Prakleidas, ousias, os, Euthy...
EDCS-71000208.summary: This inscription details the purchase of a house, mill, and other property for five talents by an individual from Dikaiagoras, with some text partially restored and several repeated date/financial indicators, likely Hellenistic.

EDCS-71000209.translation: ...Philitas. Antandros, son of Sokeus. ...esi. Philista, ...ei. ...ai. ...ai. Deinion, son of Pollias. Lysippos, son of Stilpon. Exoles. Oi...
EDCS-71000209.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that appears to list several names, possibly related to a property transaction or a list of individuals, with most context missing.

EDCS-71000224.translation: Under Dameas, son of Artamitis, on the ninth of the current month, Hestia, fifth, first, granted ninety minae of silver and one talent, interest-free, to Hieron, son of Damarkhos, concerning the house in the lane of the Lanôn. It was stated that Hieron would redeem the house from himself. Ambokhoi: Aristion, son of Diokles, first of the ninth [class]; Sosandros, son of Tharrydamas, first of the ninth [class]; Apollonidas, son of Sosandros, second of the ninth [class]; Sosandros, son of Sosios, twelfth, second. ...emos, son of Nemenios, second of the ninth [class]. Simias, first.
EDCS-71000224.summary: This inscription records Hestia granting ninety minae and a talent interest-free to Hieron for a house, with provisions for redemption, and a list of guarantors, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000228.summary: This inscription, a simple list of monetary units followed by a place name, likely records a payment or a debt related to Hydria.

EDCS-71000238.translation: Aristomachos, Apollonidas son of Phrynos from Naros, Aischylos, Pasiones wrote these down for Aristomachos, Apollodoros son of Aristomachos, Ariston son of Epigonos, and anyone else who may bear witness. Aristomachos son of Ariston and these also wrote them down for Aristomachos.
EDCS-71000238.summary: This inscription appears to be a legal or property record, detailing individuals who witnessed or recorded transactions or agreements for Aristomachos and others, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000239.translation: Dionysios son of Philinos, Phergos son of Ergoteleos, Neomenios son of Ergoteleos, Drakontios, Domios, Gigas, Simias son of Geloi.
EDCS-71000239.summary: This inscription is a simple list of names, possibly an ephebic list, a dedication, or a record of citizens, likely from the Hellenistic or Roman period.

EDCS-71000240.summary: This inscription is a short list of names, possibly indicating ownership, a dedication, or a familial relationship, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000241.summary: This inscription records the name of Archeledamos son of Damoefeides along with a numerical figure, possibly related to a count, a measurement, or a sum of money, dating from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000254.translation: Kallikrates, beautiful horse and Losais, son of Amounphios. They received sustenance to remain in memory and to be together always.
EDCS-71000254.summary: This inscription is a dedication, possibly for a horse named 'beautiful horse', and seems to commemorate individuals named Kallikrates and Losais, son of Amounphios, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000282.translation: Archelas son of Eukleides, to Dionysos.
EDCS-71000282.summary: This is a dedication of an altar or offering to the god Dionysos by Archelas son of Eukleides, likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000288.translation: All the following are on condition of release; to be released within a year or within a six-month period. They belong to Dion son of...; Apochoi: Aischrion son of Stratos, Stratius son of Aischrion, Philon, Aristarchos son of Philistion. Apochoi: Aischrion son of Stratos, Stratius son of Aischrion, ... Philon, Aristarchos son of Philistion.
EDCS-71000288.summary: This inscription details terms of release or redemption for a contract or debt, listing individuals who are 'apochoi' (guarantors or sureties), likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000289.translation: Under the hierapolos Orthos son of Theudaisios, he buys vines and all the following with the vines, and two parts of the land, and of the others, half of the entire plot, and within the others, half. Lysonas son of Hippias bought from Thestos son of Damarchos and from Satyros son of Damon, who were orphobotae (grape-pickers/overseers), except for the sacred ones. Apochoi: Nikias, Kratia, Emmenidas son of Damon, Theodoros son of Eupolemos, Philonidas, Herakleidas, Iarones son of Filiarchos, Philiarchos son of Apollonidas, Deinos, Phintias, Pyrrias son of Karaios.
EDCS-71000289.summary: This inscription records a property transaction involving the purchase of vines and land, specifying terms of sale and listing guarantors ('apochoi'), likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000290.translation: Under the hierapolos of Diogenes, in the month of Apellaios, on the eleventh day of the waxing moon. Klenagoras son of Ariston bought a house from Agatethor for... talents. Apochoi: Lissos son of Tharsynon, Iarones son of Agatharchos, Timon son of Sthenon, Dionysios son of Philinos, Hipposthenes son of Praton.
EDCS-71000290.summary: This inscription documents a real estate transaction, the purchase of a house, specifying the date, parties involved, and listing guarantors ('apochoi'), likely from the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-71000447.translation: To Zeus and Fortune, by Marcianós.
EDCS-71000447.summary: This is a dedication to Zeus and Fortune by a person named Marcianós.

EDCS-71000448.translation: Gaius Claudius, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-71000448.summary: This is a votive offering by Gaius Claudius.

EDCS-71000451.translation: Damatrios, in his third year as head attendant of the attendant Paianios, son of Theollos, and Dion, son of Aristarchos, from Erimē. Second, second, purchased from Philonis, son of Nemenios, from Perēkyata. First, tenth part for an entrance, which was assessed at a value of six talents of silver and one hundred staters. Ambokhoi, Lykinos, son of Zopyros, from Erimē. Gorgylos, son of Rhadon, from Kratemeia. First, second. Aristias, son of Straton, from Kratemeia. Second, third. Sosistratos, son of Gelon, from Perēkyata. First, second. Second, third.
EDCS-71000451.summary: This inscription appears to record a property transaction or division, detailing purchases and shares of land or goods, possibly related to a legal or administrative process, dated by the mention of specific officials or periods.

EDCS-71000452.translation: Sopatrós, son of Philon, from Perē. Philistíōn, son of Philonis. Lab... Agelokhos, son of Nikolokhos, from Perē. Panokharíōn, son of Armateos, from Lab... Andreas, son of Thēon, from Plē. Menestratos, son of Menekles, from Nēt. Onator, son of Mnámōn, from Erimē. Aristias, son of Nikōn, from Plē. Zopyros, son of Damokrates, from Log... Ariston, son of Leon, from Arch... Antallos, son of Sopatros, from Erimē. Pyrrhos, son of Sopatros, from Mak... Aristoxenos, Apollodoros, from Kra... Philon, son of Konkhōn. Sósandros, son of Artamidōros, from Plē. Blosōn, son of Philon, from Mak... Gorgias, Olumbios, from Hypha... Lycidas, son of Nikostratos, from Nēt. Histiodoros, son of Dorotheos, from Nēt. Aischríōn, son of Hermōn, from Mak... Phintías, son of Agatharhos, from Nēt. Timasíōn, son of Aristokles, from Plē. Zopyros, son of Dion, from Lab... Thēodoros, son of Pammakhos, from Mak... Sopatros, son of Sosibios, from Plē. Eudamos, son of Sosipatrós, from Mak... Dionysios, son of Thiōn, from Nēt. Nikaios, son of Pollyllos, from Hypha... Menekleidas, Mettios, from Lab... Sōsis, son of Hermōn, from Plē. Kallistratos, son of Khairēmon, from Mak... Sōmēdēs, son of Sōtairos, from Plē. Philokrates, son of Philon, from Plē. Philoxenos, son of Sōsia, from Hypha... Leanax, son of Tharsagoras, from Hypha... Hiaron, son of Simos, from Plē. Zopyros, son of Dion, from Lab... Zopyros, son of Kleidamos, from Hypha... Thrasōn, son of Herakleidas, from Hypha... Euthyboulos, son of Euthymidas, from Hypha... Artamidōros, son of Sōtadas, from Hypha... Artamidōros, son of Philonis, from Mak... Diokles, son of Kephalos, from Lab... Phintías, son of Philonis, from Hypha... Alexis, son of Tēlegonos, from Log... Kallias, son of Aristokles, from Tēl... Apollodoros, son of Nemenios, from Phel... Polyxenos, son of Thrasys, from Mak... Deinōn, son of Simias, from Hypha... Mousaios, son of Arethōn, from Mak... Hermonax, son of Alkia.
EDCS-71000452.summary: This inscription is a list of names, likely recording members of a group, association, or citizens, possibly related to a civic or religious body, with patronymics and possibly origins or tribal affiliations indicated.

EDCS-71000483.translation: To Thōas, Sostratos, Iaron, Hermodoros, Hieronymos, Aristis, Sinaras, Lysanias, Athanis, Sosimos.
EDCS-71000483.summary: This inscription is a list of names, possibly a dedication or a record of individuals present at an event.

EDCS-71000506.translation: Under Philokrates, Annaus. Theodoros, son of Artemon, logistes. Under Apellios, son of Artemon, An... Artemon, son of Artemon, logistes. Under Apellios. Under Dionysios, son of Herakleios. Good, until...
EDCS-71000506.summary: This inscription records individuals holding office, possibly administrative or financial roles (logistes), under specific officials named Philokrates and Apellios, with a mention of Dionysios. It seems to be a record of tenure or a decree related to their service.

EDCS-71000550.summary: This inscription is very fragmentary but appears to be a personal name or a title followed by an unclear word, possibly Latin 'veni' (came) or Greek. Its purpose is uncertain due to the brevity and damage.

EDCS-71000560.translation: To register him and his exemption. To register him and his exemption. To register him and his exemption. To register him and his exemption. Perithymos and his exemption. Yousion and him and his exemption. And him and his exemption. Poly and him and his exemption. Arothin and his exemption. Selinis and his exemption. Patron and him and his exemption. Hermon and him and his exemption. Hagesandros and him and his exemption. Pythin and him and his exemption. Straton and him and his exemption. Ameinis and him and his exemption. And him and his exemption. And him and his exemption. And his exemption. Sonautia, to register. Leptina, to register. Psokle and him and his exemption. A and him and his exemption. Sa and him and his exemption. Ain and his exemption. Ar and him and his exemption. Ka and him and his exemption. Kalliope and him and his exemption and the... Olyrikos, Pollion and Eukl...
EDCS-71000560.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of individuals and their exemptions from some form of obligation or tax, with the repeated phrase 'to register him and his exemption'. It is likely a record from an administrative or legal context.

EDCS-71000561.translation: To register the decree of Eukles. Do not register anything for Mestor; it will benefit the gloss of Simias, son of Mikythos. Do not register anything for Mestor; it will benefit the gloss of Pithaoqos. To register the decree... Do not register anything for Mestor; it will benefit the decree of Forlilos. Do not register anything for Mestor; it will benefit Philondas, son of Khoirinas, and Polykles. May they not register anything for Mestor; it will benefit the gloss of Mestor, son of Vikellos. To register all the glosses of the friends of Vikellos. For the glosses, may they not register anything for Mestor; it will benefit the archistrategos, son of Aischinas, in place of whom they owed glosses to Mestor.
EDCS-71000561.summary: This inscription details the registration of decrees and glosses (interpretations or records) related to individuals like Mestor, Eukles, Simias, Pithaoqos, Philondas, Polykles, and Aischinas, possibly concerning legal matters or official pronouncements.

EDCS-71000576.translation: Under the hieromnamon Leukios, son of Pakkios Eumenideios, on the sixth day. It was decided by the assembly, as it was also by the council, that since the Geloi, when they previously inhabited this city, when war broke out against the Carthaginians, assisted our city with cavalry and infantry, and became allies and contributed to an alliance. And similarly, now, since we have been inhabiting the city, making it clear that they remember the friendship and goodwill they have shown towards us, it has been decided to inscribe on bronze, after writing down in the sacred precinct of Hestia the goodwill and alliance that exists between us and the Geloi, and to invite them to the games and to offer sacrifices with them, so that we may make it clear to future generations that we remember those Geloi who provided necessities for our city.
EDCS-71000576.summary: This is a decree from the city of Gela, passed by the assembly and council, to honor the Geloi for their past and present military assistance against the Carthaginians by inscribing their alliance and goodwill on bronze in the temple of Hestia and inviting them to games and sacrifices. It is dated by the hieromnamon Leukios, son of Pakkios, to the sixth day of his term.

EDCS-71000577.translation: Under the hieromnemon Leukios son of Pakkios Eumenides, on the sixth day of the tenth [month], it was decreed by the assembly, as also by the council, that since the Herbitans, when we formerly settled this city, during the war that broke out with the Carthaginians, assisted our city and also became allies, and similarly now, since we settled the city, they make it evident that they remember the friendship and goodwill they have towards us, it has been decided to dedicate on bronze, after inscribing, in the temple of Hestia, the goodwill and alliance that exists between us and the Herbitans, and to invite them to the games and to offer sacrifices with them, in order that we may make it clear to those who come after that we remember the services rendered to our city by the Herbitans who provided assistance.
EDCS-71000577.summary: This decree from the assembly and council honors the Herbitans for their past and present assistance to the city, granting them continued alliance and participation in festivals, to be inscribed on bronze in the temple of Hestia.

EDCS-71000578.translation: Under Leukios son of Pakkios Eumenides, on the sixth day of the tenth [month], it was decreed by the assembly, as also by the council, that since in former times we had citizenship in common with the Assorini, and similarly now, as our city is being settled, they provide assistance according to their ability, it has been decided that for the Assorini, by the people of the Entellinans, the citizenship that existed from the beginning, and friendship and goodwill, shall continue, and they shall be invited to the games, and this decree, inscribed by the magistrates on bronze in the temple of Hestia, shall serve as a reminder to posterity of the existing goodwill and citizenship with the Assorini.
EDCS-71000578.summary: A decree by the assembly and council grants the Assorini continued common citizenship, friendship, and goodwill with the Entellinans, and invites them to games, to be recorded on bronze in the temple of Hestia.

EDCS-71000579.translation: Under the archons Kipos son of Soios and Theodoros son of Mamus Eumenides, on the thirtieth day, it was decreed by the council, since Tiberius Claudius son of Gaius Antiates, having been appointed supervisor of our city, has provided many great services to the community and has been a good man in the resettlement of the city at all times, it has been decided that he and his children shall be proxenoi (public guests) of the people of the Entellinans, so that others may also know that our people can adequately repay the merits of good men, and the magistrates shall inscribe this decree on bronze and dedicate it in the council chamber. It was decreed by the assembly on the fourth day of Euernes.
EDCS-71000579.summary: This decree from the council and assembly honors Tiberius Claudius Antiates for his significant contributions to the city's resettlement by appointing him and his children as proxenoi (public guests) of the Entellinans.

EDCS-71000580.translation: Under the archons Artemidoros son of Iielos and Gnaios Oppios son of Panamos, in the month of Nemenias, since some of the citizens assisted the city during its resettlement with grain, some donating it as a gift, others measuring it out, and showed great goodwill towards the people of the Entellinans, and instead of a scarcity of grain, brought about abundance, it has been decreed by the council that all who assisted the city with grain, money, or anything else during the resettlement shall have goodwill and common citizenship with the people of the Entellinans at all times, and shall be invited to the games, and shall have precedence. Also, those private individuals who assisted the city with grain during the resettlement shall be proxenoi (public guests) of the city of the Entellinans, both they and their children. It was also decreed by the assembly on Petrinus: The community measured out fifty spyrides and one hundred medimnoi. The community of Kyatattarinia gave a gift of ten spyrides and fifty [measures] of barley. The community of Scherines gave a gift of ten [spyrides] and ten [measures] of barley. Makellina's community measured out one hundred spyrides and [some] medimnoi. The private individuals of Petrina who measured out are these: Theodoros son of Praton of Sannios, fifty spyrides and one hundred [measures]; Aischylis son of Praton of Sannios, ten [spyrides] and ten [measures]; Herakleios son of Herakleidas, one hundred spyrides; Arimnastos son of Simos, fifty [measures]; Sosandros son of Ariston, fifty spyrides; Minatos son of Koruos, the Mamertean, ten [measures] of barley. And the magistrates shall inscribe this decree on bronze and dedicate it in the council chamber.
EDCS-71000580.summary: This decree from the council and assembly grants citizenship, goodwill, and honors to individuals and communities who provided grain or other aid during the city's resettlement, to be inscribed on bronze in the council chamber.

EDCS-71000581.translation: Under the archons Artemidoros son of Iielos and Gnaios Oppios son of Panamos, in the month of Nemenias, since the Ennaeans have been consistently well-disposed towards us both in private and when we were exiled from our private lives and wandered, seeking help, and welcomed us to their city and land, it has been decreed by the council and the assembly that they shall have goodwill and common citizenship with the people of the Entellinans at all times. And the magistrates shall inscribe this decree on bronze and dedicate it in the council chamber.
EDCS-71000581.summary: A decree from the council and assembly grants the Ennaeans perpetual goodwill and common citizenship with the Entellinans for their consistent support and assistance during times of exile.

EDCS-71000582.translation: Under the archons Artemidoros son of Iielos and Gnaios Oppios son of Panamos, in the month of Nemenias, since the Egestans have been consistently well-disposed towards us, both when we were in private and when we were driven out of our private lives, and when many citizens, men and women, were captured, they assisted them as they were able, so that they might quickly return to their private lives, it has been decreed by the council and the assembly that they shall have goodwill and common citizenship with the people of the Entellinans at all times. And the magistrates shall inscribe this decree on bronze and dedicate it in the council chamber.
EDCS-71000582.summary: This decree from the council and assembly grants the Egestans perpetual goodwill and common citizenship with the Entellinans for their consistent support and aid during times of hardship and capture of citizens.

EDCS-71000659.summary: This is a dedication or naming inscription, possibly for a tomb or a statue, mentioning the name Menekrates Olympios Mousimas.

EDCS-71000681.translation: The architect Proxenus son of Loiskos the Thermian.
EDCS-71000681.summary: This is an inscription identifying Proxenus, son of Loiskos, as an architect from Thermos.

EDCS-71000716.summary: This inscription appears to record a date, specifically the month of Heraios.

EDCS-71000769.translation: Prigomenia, good, blameless, faithful, and inimitable, lived [number] years.
EDCS-71000769.summary: This is an epitaph for Prigomenia, praising her virtues and recording her lifespan.

EDCS-71000772.translation: Petronia lived blamelessly and with dignity for 12 years, 4 months, and 4 days. Gaius Cornelius, father, made it for his beloved daughter.
EDCS-71000772.summary: A funerary monument for Petronia, set up by her father, Gaius Cornelius, around the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-71000776.translation: Scribonia, while alive, made this for herself and all her own people.
EDCS-71000776.summary: A funerary monument made by Scribonia for herself and her family, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-71000777.translation: Zacharias and Quintus, his son, made this in memory of his co-wife and mother, Ichnatia.
EDCS-71000777.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Ichnatia by her husband Zacharias and son Quintus, probably from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-71000778.translation: For the Chthonic Gods. Julia Gamike Ichnatia made this memorial for her husband, who lived 53 years, out of goodwill and remembrance.
EDCS-71000778.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to the Chthonic Gods and a husband by his wife, Julia Gamike Ichnatia, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-71000779.translation: For the Chthonic Gods, for the Daimones. Peskennia Sikela Drepanis, a Christian, lived blamelessly for [number] years. Aurelius Simbius made this for his most pious wife, with devotion and remembrance.
EDCS-71000779.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to the Chthonic Gods and Daimones, set up by Aurelius Simbius for his wife Peskennia Sikela Drepanis, who is identified as Christian and lived blamelessly.

EDCS-71000780.translation: For all virtue, wisdom, and piety. He made and renovated this in memory of Claudius Gaius.
EDCS-71000780.summary: An inscription recording the dedication and renovation of something in memory of Claudius Gaius, likely a public or private monument from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-71000784.translation: For all the gods, saviors of the city of Drepanon, Lucius Mallius, chiliarch, [set up] the table and the altar.
EDCS-71000784.summary: A dedication of an altar and table to the savior gods of Drepanon by Lucius Mallius, a chiliarch, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71000785.translation: For the Archegetes, for Heracles Thallophorus, the most holy. Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius Cornelius Terentianus, and Lemnius, a freedman, made [this].
EDCS-71000785.summary: A dedication to the Archegetes and the holy Heracles Thallophorus by Lucius Cornelius (son of Lucius Cornelius Terentianus) and a freedman named Lemnius, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-71200472.translation: Nicomedes, also called Onyx, for Sotira.
EDCS-71200472.summary: A dedication or memorial inscription for Sotira, by Nicomedes (also known as Onyx), probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300108.translation: Anaximander, son of Praxiades, of Miletus, was born [during the time] of Thales.
EDCS-72300108.summary: An epitaph identifying Anaximander of Miletus and noting his birth during the time of Thales, likely dating to the Hellenistic or Roman period.

EDCS-72300109.translation: Callisthenes of Olynthus became a letter-writer for Alexander, and he left behind writings recording the deeds of...
EDCS-72300109.summary: This inscription is a fragment of a biographical notice of Callisthenes, a writer associated with Alexander the Great, dating likely to the Hellenistic period.

EDCS-72300110.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a proper name from Elis, with no further context.

EDCS-72300111.translation: Quintus Fabius Pictorinus, called Romanus, son of Gaius, recorded the arrival of Hercules in Italy and... Later Romulus and Remus came into being, and the founding of Rome by Romulus... Philistus of Syracuse is said to have been a student of the one who first wrote elegies, and to have first established the currently prosperous way of life, and to have divided the day correctly and to have begun...
EDCS-72300111.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary historical account, likely from a public monument, attributing the writing of early Roman history and traditions to Quintus Fabius Pictor, and referencing Philistus of Syracuse.

EDCS-72300129.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or a dedication fragment, possibly from a statue base or similar monument, mentioning 'Philone of Sycae'.

EDCS-72300157.translation: Nicomachus, son of Simos, of the eleventh phratry, first decas, seventh...
EDCS-72300157.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly recording a tribal or phratry list or a civic registration, mentioning Nicomachus and his affiliation.

EDCS-73400292.translation: Sopos [dedicated] his mother Phalacria, to Phalacria, for goodwill.
EDCS-73400292.summary: This is a dedication by Sopos to his mother Phalacria, likely on a tomb or a monument honoring her, motivated by affection.

EDCS-73400297.translation: Second tribe, phratry... Polyxenos, son of Arkesilas.
EDCS-73400297.summary: This inscription fragment appears to record a tribal and phratry affiliation, possibly for an individual named Polyxenos.

EDCS-73500001.translation: Cobuldeus and Maxima fulfilled a vow for their own salvation and that of their children.
EDCS-73500001.summary: This is a votive inscription where Cobuldeus and Maxima made a dedication to secure their own and their children's well-being.

EDCS-73500002.translation: Remember your servant Sapricius, O Christ.
EDCS-73500002.summary: This is an early Christian inscription, a prayer for remembrance for a deceased person named Sapricius, likely from a tomb.

EDCS-73500003.translation: Macarius the elder made [this] for the salvation of Cobuldeus.
EDCS-73500003.summary: This is a votive or funerary inscription by Macarius, indicating a dedication or prayer for the salvation of Cobuldeus.

EDCS-73900014.summary: This is a dedication to Felicis, Bishop of Panormus, likely from a tomb or church.

EDCS-73900019.translation: Hermas the saint, of good memory, was laid to rest in this place; he completed about 52 years of his life. Death carried him off on the Kalends of November, on a Tuesday, the 12th day after the New Moon.
EDCS-73900019.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Hermas, noting his age and the date of his passing.

EDCS-74100026.translation: Gaius Fannius Minatus, son of Ceives, and the Frentani who live in Sicily, have seen to the building of a shrine to Hercules Novitanus, with money collected amounting to 643 and a half sesterces.
EDCS-74100026.summary: This inscription records the construction of a shrine to Hercules Novitanus by Gaius Fannius Minatus and the Frentani people in Sicily, detailing the cost.

EDCS-75400005.translation: The people of Egesta to Phaon, son of Nymphon, for his virtue.
EDCS-75400005.summary: This is a civic dedication from the people of Egesta to Phaon, honoring him for his virtue.

EDCS-75400042.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a personal dedication or a tombstone, with only the patronymic and partial name preserved.

EDCS-76300115.summary: This is a dedication to Pediakrates, identified as a hero, possibly on a tomb or a cultic monument.

EDCS-76300120.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Gaius Claudius.

EDCS-76300121.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Zosimus, son of Zosimus, likely on a tombstone or ownership marker.

EDCS-76300122.translation: To Jupiter and Fortune, from Marcianus.
EDCS-76300122.summary: This is a dedication by Marcianus to Jupiter and Fortune.

EDCS-76300172.translation: To all the gods, the people of Halaesa, from Apollodoros, son of Sal, to Lapiro, for goodwill and beneficence towards him.
EDCS-76300172.summary: This inscription is a civic dedication from the people of Halaesa, likely honoring Lapiro for his good deeds and benefactions, possibly related to Apollodoros.

EDCS-76300173.translation: To all the gods, the soldiers who served by sea, the people of Halaisa, Kalakte, Herbitai, and Amestratinai, in recognition of goodwill.
EDCS-76300173.summary: This is a dedication to all gods by soldiers and various communities, likely a votive inscription erected due to goodwill.

EDCS-76300185.translation: Melampus, son of Eupolemos, died at the age of nine.
EDCS-76300185.summary: This is a funerary epigram recording the death of a young boy named Melampus.

EDCS-76400128.translation: Hephaestus, Dionysus, Sikon, Hygieion, Kalē, Kalē, Kalē, Kalē.
EDCS-76400128.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly deities or individuals, perhaps related to a cult or a dedication.

EDCS-76400181.translation: To the Chthonic gods. Arrius Koprianos lived for 4 years, a foster-child of Strymphakos.
EDCS-76400181.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Chthonic gods, recording the short life of Arrius Koprianos.

EDCS-76400186.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating supervision or management by Artemidoros, possibly on a building or project.

EDCS-76500057.summary: This inscription simply repeats a name, Koisyra, possibly a dedication, ownership mark, or a simple memorial.

EDCS-76800139.summary: This is a simple greeting to an individual named Philokles, possibly inscribed on a tomb or as a dedication.

EDCS-76800144.translation: Euthyros, son of Euthoynos, fourth (year?), eternally youthful. Euthyros.
EDCS-76800144.summary: This inscription seems to be a funerary epitaph for Euthyros, possibly mentioning his youth and perhaps a date.

EDCS-76900077.summary: This inscription contains two names, 'beautiful Rhīnon' and 'Leagros', possibly a dedication or a statement of affection.

EDCS-77300002.translation: Deianira, Nikē, Herakles, Achelōos, Oineus, Thalia, Simos, Skirtos.
EDCS-77300002.summary: This inscription is a list of names, possibly deities, mythological figures, or individuals, perhaps related to a cult or a patronymic dedication.

EDCS-77300003.summary: This is a Greek funerary inscription identifying an individual named Opōra Odysseus Marōn, possibly with a status designation.

EDCS-78000007.summary: This inscription records lead belonging to Caius Laberius Atictus, likely related to a property or construction project.

EDCS-78500039.translation: Soter lies here, having lived his life blamelessly. He died on the 18th of August, having lived 50 years, more or less, after the consulship of Theodosius for the 12th time and Valentinianus for the 2nd time. Peace to your soul.
EDCS-78500039.summary: This is a Greek funerary inscription for Soter, who died on August 18th and lived approximately 50 years, with a date referencing the consulship of Theodosius and Valentinianus in the 5th century CE.

EDCS-78700088.translation: Ianuarius lies here, having lived virtuously and blamelessly for 60 years, and for 44 years as a priest in Logarianis.
EDCS-78700088.summary: This Greek funerary inscription records the life of Ianuarius, who lived 60 years and served as a priest for 44 years in Logarianis.

EDCS-79600013.translation: Of Marcus Octavius, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-79600013.summary: This is a Latin ownership inscription, likely referring to property or a tomb belonging to Marcus Octavius, a freedman.

EDCS-79600014.translation: Of Marcus Octavius, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-79600014.summary: This is a Latin ownership inscription, likely referring to property or a tomb belonging to Marcus Octavius, a freedman.

EDCS-79600015.translation: Of Marcus Octavius, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-79600015.summary: This is a Latin ownership inscription, likely referring to property or a tomb belonging to Marcus Octavius, a freedman.

EDCS-79600016.translation: Of Gnaeus Atellius, freedman of Gnaeus.
EDCS-79600016.summary: This is a Latin ownership inscription, likely referring to property or a tomb belonging to Gnaeus Atellius, a freedman.

EDCS-79600017.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius Russinus.
EDCS-79600017.summary: This is a Latin ownership inscription, likely referring to property or a tomb belonging to Marcus Planius Russinus.

EDCS-79600018.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius Russinus.
EDCS-79600018.summary: This is a Latin ownership inscription, likely referring to property or a tomb belonging to Marcus Planius Russinus.

EDCS-79600019.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius, Russinus.
EDCS-79600019.summary: This is a funerary or ownership inscription identifying Marcus Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-79600020.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius, Russinus.
EDCS-79600020.summary: This is a funerary or ownership inscription identifying Marcus Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-79600021.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius, Russinus.
EDCS-79600021.summary: This is a funerary or ownership inscription identifying Marcus Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-79600022.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius, Russinus.
EDCS-79600022.summary: This is a funerary or ownership inscription identifying Marcus Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-79600023.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius, Russinus.
EDCS-79600023.summary: This is a funerary or ownership inscription identifying Marcus Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-79600024.translation: Of Marcus Planius, son of Lucius, Russinus.
EDCS-79600024.summary: This is a funerary or ownership inscription identifying Marcus Planius Russinus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-80700015.translation: Archela, daughter of Eukleides, to Dionysus.
EDCS-80700015.summary: This is a dedication to the god Dionysus by Archela, daughter of Eukleides.

EDCS-80700018.summary: This is a funerary marker identifying a tomb belonging to Alexios.

EDCS-80700019.translation: Here lies Ilasios, who lived 30 years and died three days before the Ides of September; peace to you in Christ.
EDCS-80700019.summary: This is a Christian epitaph recording the death of Ilasios at age 30, with a date and a Christian salutation.

EDCS-80700024.translation: Gnaeus, son of Gnaeus, Marcus, greetings.
EDCS-80700024.summary: This is a personal greeting inscription, likely for a tomb or marker, addressed to Gnaeus Marcus, son of Gnaeus.

EDCS-80700026.translation: To Zeus and Tyche, from Markianos.
EDCS-80700026.summary: A dedication to Zeus and Tyche by Markianos.

EDCS-80700027.translation: Gaius Claudius, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-80700027.summary: A vow fulfillment by Gaius Claudius.

EDCS-80700054.translation: To all the gods, the people of Halaes demonstrates its goodwill and beneficence towards Apollodoros, son of Sal., for his kindness and beneficence towards it.
EDCS-80700054.summary: The people of Halaes express their goodwill and beneficence towards Apollodoros, son of Sal., likely on a public monument.

EDCS-80700061.translation: I am the tomb of Krates, belonging to Eupylos.
EDCS-80700061.summary: An epitaph for Krates, indicating ownership by Eupylos.

EDCS-80700078.translation: During the gymnasium directorship of Onasikrates, son of Onasikrates, and Zoilus, son of Nymphon, and Artamon, son of Archonidas, the young men in Bassanos [---] Agatharch[---]
EDCS-80700078.summary: A dedication or record of an event related to the gymnasium directorship and the young men of Bassanos during a specific period.

EDCS-80700079.translation: Here lie Choroi and Apelos, who received the lot of death; their dear son buried both.
EDCS-80700079.summary: An epitaph for Choroi and Apelos, buried by their son.

EDCS-80700081.summary: An identification of Philistidas as a potter, possibly associated with Euxenos.

EDCS-80700082.translation: Tinasinos, Oimtis, Kaposos, Damon, Sosias, Neias, Alkia, Biotos, Deinos, Gelios.
EDCS-80700082.summary: A list of names, possibly related to a property or a group, with unclear context.

EDCS-80700134.translation: Islanos, son of Hagesistratos; Teisias, son of Denia; Hippon, son of Hechekleis; Kaias, son of Silanos; Hagesstratos, son of Koleoxnos; Ischyros, son of Schaios; Kleidas, son of Gorgias; Tiatas, son of Ismos; Hagesstros, son of Idodoros; Diona; Inkomakos; Uekleia; Islanos, son of Epikydes.
EDCS-80700134.summary: A long list of names, primarily patronymics, possibly recording a group of citizens, landowners, or members of a guild.

EDCS-82000004.summary: A funerary inscription for a faithful person, likely dating from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-82000005.translation: Felix the faithful bought a place for myself from the deacon in peace.
EDCS-82000005.summary: A funerary inscription recording the purchase of a burial plot by Felix the faithful from a deacon, likely dating from the 4th to 6th centuries CE.

EDCS-82000104.translation: In the time of Aleximachos, son of Smithos.
EDCS-82000104.summary: An inscription indicating a date or period of office under Aleximachos, son of Smithos, potentially from the Greek-speaking eastern Roman Empire.
